item.description: SNY Knicks: "I saw he wasn't looking so I just tried to go get it”. Jalen Brunson talks about his steal in the final seconds leading to his free throws:

item.title: “It just speaks to his resiliency … making the biggest …
item.description: Ian Begley: “It just speaks to his resiliency … making the biggest play in the game,” Karl-Anthony Towns on Mitchell Robinson stopping Victor Wembanyama twice in final 30 seconds. Robinson: “Been here for 8 years, now here we are in the Finals. I can truly say I done seen it all.”

item.title: Joe Clements happened to be sitting near Sandra …
item.description: A man went viral after thanking Jalen Brunson’s mom for creating the Knicks star. Joe Clements happened to be sitting near Sandra Brunson in the stands in San Antonio after Game 1 of the NBA Finals — so he used the moment to express his gratitude. “Hey, Mrs. Brunson, Thank you so much for having that boy. He is the franchise changer. We love him,” Clements said in the video, which racked up 1.7 million views, and hundreds of comments from fans, calling her the “Queen of NYC.”

item.title: The 7-foot-1 Luke Kornet bent down to the 5-foot-1 …
item.description: The 7-foot-1 Luke Kornet bent down to the 5-foot-1 sister, and she placed her hand on his head. They each closed their eyes. She prayed for the holy spirit to be with him. The moment went viral. The Spurs won. Kornet, who finished with six points, seven rebounds and two blocks off the bench, said afterward that the sisters needed to be in Oklahoma City for Game 6. "We've got to get 'em on the plane," he said.

item.title: Wembanyama came out last again and again acknowledged …
item.description: Thanks to another invite from the Kahlig Auto Group, four sisters attended Wednesday's Game 1 loss to the Knicks. Before the game, the sisters once again gathered in the tunnel. Kornet, after passing them by, came back and thanked them for being there. Wembanyama came out last again and again acknowledged them. Soon after, they were standing courtside when a familiar voice asked them to turn around. "Sisters, could you please pray for me?" Wembanyama asked them.

item.title: Danny Green: Uncle Dennis had a lot to do with Kawhi Leonard's eventual departure from San Antonio
item.description: Danny Green on Uncle Dennis' influence on Kawhi Leonard leaving San Antonio: I think his Uncle had a lot of what to do, what’s going on off the court, a lot of influence. I think once his Uncle saw some distasteful things in his mind. It was like, “Alright we not doing this.” Because San Antonio they don’t treat nobody like superstars and I think his Uncle kind of rightfully so, you know what I’m saying? should have been treated like a superstar.

item.title: Knicks' interest in Giannis Antetokounmpo could increase if they lose in Finals?
item.description: Heat Central: Chris Haynes on Giannis: “I think this is the closest he’s come to saying he wants to be moved without saying he wants to be moved. I think signs are pointing to that…. This game (finals) is going to have some implications as well. If the Knicks were to falter to the Spurs, they might be in play for Giannis.”

item.title: On Luke Kornet kick ball late, NBA says: “Although …
item.description: Ian Begley: In its 2-minute report, NBA finds that referees in Game 2 of Knicks-Spurs made no errors in final 2 minutes. Every call was either a correct call or a correct non-call. On Luke Kornet kick ball late, NBA says: “Although Kornet (SAS) lifts his foot, he does not intentionally kick the loose ball.”

item.title: NBA rescinds technical foul on Mitchell Robinson for dustup with Victor Wembanyama
item.description: Chris Haynes: NBA has rescinded the technical foul Mitchell Robinson received after a back and forth shoving match with Victor Wembanyama last night. Wemby did not receive a tech.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson's (NYK) technical foul against …
item.description: NBA Official: Mitchell Robinson's (NYK) technical foul against Victor Wembanyama (SAS) at 4:56 of the 2nd quarter in Game 2 of the NBA Finals on 6/5/26 has been rescinded upon league review.

item.title: Along the way, with Karl-Anthony Towns leading the …
item.description: Along the way, with Karl-Anthony Towns leading the Knicks in playoff plus-minus while averaging 17.3 points, 10.8 rebounds and 5.6 assists in the playoffs, it has become quite evident that he deserves the kind of respect that some of his peers have been loath to give over the years (from Jimmy Butler on down the line). His defense against Spurs big man Victor Wembanyama in this series’ first two games should be enough to reshape how he’s viewed. That this revelatory showing comes after Randle st

item.title: What’s scary is that there is another level the Knicks …
item.description: What’s scary is that there is another level the Knicks can reach. Jalen Brunson in particular. He has been far from his best across the first two games of the Finals — he has shot just 19-for-56 from the field (33.9 percent) and 4-for-17 from 3-point range (23.5 percent). He’s committed a combined eight turnovers. In Friday’s 105-104 Game 2 win over the Spurs at Frost Bank Center, he missed three straight shots as the Spurs went on a 14-0 run to tie the game late in the fourth quarter.

item.title: “Don’t be afraid to fail.” I’ve always been afraid to …
item.description: Watching Jalen Brunson relentlessly try to will New York back into games in fourth quarters, not caring if one shot doesn’t go in because he trusts his work has been a reminder to me to believe in myself and my track record. I’ve been doing this a long time with a lot to show for it. Watching Mike Brown tinker with lineups, strategies and philosophies from one game to the next has made think, “Don’t be afraid to fail.” I’ve always been afraid to fail. “It’s all about being able to say you gave i

item.title: Guard Jose Alvarado, a trade deadline acquisition, has …
item.description: But under coach Mike Brown this season, New York has leaned on its bench more than it ever did with Thibodeau. That's especially true in this playoff run, which is headed back to MSG for Monday's Game 3 of the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs (8:30 p.m. ET, ABC). Up and down the Knicks bench is evidence of a pie being divided more evenly: Beyond Shamet's contributions, guard Miles McBride has averaged career highs in minutes, scoring and 3-point percentage. Big man Mitchell Robinson has stay

item.title: “It was huge,” Brown said. “Pop — I talk about MSG …
item.description: Brown has coached some of the league’s biggest stars, including LeBron James and Kobe Bryant. He coached under some of the most venerated coaches, including Popovich and Steve Kerr. But now, for the first time in his career, the Larry O’Brien Trophy is within his grasp as the head of a locker room. Brown points to his time under Popovich as one of the biggest reasons he has arrived at this moment. “It was huge,” Brown said. “Pop — I talk about MSG being iconic, New York City being iconic — Pop i

item.title: Nick Van Exel says Kobe Bryant outworked Dirk Nowitzki and Manu Ginobili: 'It wasn’t even close'
item.description: Nick Van Exel: "I've been around some incredible workers. I played with Manu Ginobili. I played with Dirk Nowitzki. Those guys were among the hardest workers I've ever seen in basketball. But Kobe Bryant was on another level. When it came to work ethic, he ran laps around people. It wasn't even close. That's not disrespect toward Manu or Dirk. It's a compliment to Kobe. His commitment was extreme. His attention to detail was obsessive. Even as an 18-year-old, he approached improvement differentl

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns on Victor Wembanyama: 'It's great when you can get to go against a great player'
item.description: Tom Petrini: Karl-Anthony Towns with the utmost respect for Victor Wembanyama: “It's great when you get to go against a great player, a player that I've spoken about countless times, that's going to be a generational player, something we've never seen in NBA before. So to be able to go and play in this moment against the best is all you can ask for. And I think for us to be in this position right now at 2-0 speaks to our team's resiliency, grit, and what we've been able to do collectively as a g

item.title: Stephon Castle on Gregg Popovich speaking to team …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Stephon Castle on Gregg Popovich speaking to team before it left for NYC: "Pretty much his message was just let the last two games go. It happened. They were very winnable games. We feel like we gave them those games. We got to let those go and focus on the next one."

item.title: Stephon Castle on his sore right ankle: "It's been …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Stephon Castle on his sore right ankle: "It's been feeling good...It feels a lot better than I thought it would initially. Obviously, I had some adrenaline running, kind of once I settled down, I started to feel it. Waking up the next day, actually felt really good."

item.title: Julian Champagnie on NYC reception: 'We get some hoorays, we get some boos, some middle fingers'
item.description: Tom Orsborn: NYC native Julian Champagnie on the vibe in the city: “The vibes have been great. They've been crazy. Get off the bus, fans are there. We get some hoorays, we get some boos, some middle fingers, stuff like that. But that's just New York.”

item.title: VJ Edgecombe on Victor Wembanyama: Seeing him in person, he's not real
item.description: Is Victor Wembanyama even more ridiculous in person? VJ Edgecombe: Yeah, he's insane. Obviously, there's never been anyone around that's obviously as skilled as him at that height, so, yeah. I've seen him in person. I just think when you see somebody like that like in the court, what is this? Yeah, man. He's not real. I don't think he's real. [Laughs]

item.title: Victor Wembanya on Spurs being in the Finals: We're like spoiled kids to be in the Finals this early in our careers
item.description: “They (the Knicks) understand how fortunate they are to be in the Finals, and they know it's not guaranteed they'll ever get another chance like this," the 22-year-old star told a French reporter in their native language. "As for us, we're kind of like spoiled kids," Wembanyama added. "For some of us, it's our first season, or one of our first seasons, and we're already in the Finals. We don't fully realize it yet. And to me, the team that appreciates the position we're in the most will be the o

item.title: "So, I don't think it's necessarily a mentality where …
item.description: Spurs elder statesman Harrison Barnes has a message for his younger teammates: Don’t take being in the NBA Finals for granted. “You have no idea” what's going to happen next season, the 14th-year forward said. “Every single year you have to begin at the start of the journey, the start of the mountain and climb, and health is a major part of it," he added. "So, I don't think it's necessarily a mentality where we say, ‘This is their only shot’ or ‘Just because we're young, we have a lot of differe

item.title: "Not really. It could be, but isolating myself from …
item.description: Maxime Aubin: Is Victor Wembanyama expecting a more difficult task in New York with the increased media attention and, in particular, the expected presence of Donald Trump at Game 3? "Not really. It could be, but isolating myself from the outside noise is something I've practiced over the years. I think I'm good at it. So it's not a problem."

item.title: I think for me I feel like I'm at my best when I play …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Dylan Harper's mindset for tomorrow: "Desperation. I think for me I feel like I'm at my best when I play with that type of desperation and I think that's what's kind of needed for this occasion. I feel like I'm going to go out there and no matter what the circumstances, I think if I'm not playing with that type of fire or grit, I'm doing a disservice to everyone"

item.title: No injuries to report for the Spurs going into Game 3.
item.description: Michael C. Wright: No injuries to report for the Spurs going into Game 3. Was wondering whether Steph Castle or De'Aaron Fox would be on there.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on trying to come back from a 0-2 deficit: There's no reason to overthink it, this is what I'm built for
item.description: Jared Weiss: Victor Wembanyama on the pressure of trying to come back from down 0-2 in the NBA Finals: “How do I deal with it? I think the key is acceptance a lot of times, taking a step back, realizing all the journey that's behind this and what's ahead of this. Just being okay with who I am, where I am, what I'm doing. I think this is everything that I wished for. There's really no reason to overthink it. I mean, this is what I'm built for.”

item.title: The NBA has just notified us that the locker rooms of …
item.description: Maxime Aubin: Not only will Donald Trump's presence affect the fans' experience at Madison Square Garden tomorrow night, but also our work, we journalists. The NBA has just notified us that the locker rooms of both teams will not be open to the press before the game, no more than the sidelines of the court starting 1h15 before it begins. Accesses that are generally essential for obtaining good testimonials, networking, and enriching our articles.

item.title: “He’s always played years beyond his age, and he …
item.description: What a story that would be for the Harpers, from the boys’ father, five-time NBA champion Ron Sr., to their youngest sister, Mia, the undisputed best athlete of the family, for proud mother Maria and her two sons. Separated by six years but bound by the tightest of heartstrings, all eyes right now are on 20-year-old Dylan. The No. 2 pick of the 2025 draft, Dylan’s 10-point outburst off the bench in the first quarter of Game 1 made him the youngest player to record 10-plus points in an NBA Finals

item.title: A video has surfaced of Wemby sitting in a park in New …
item.description: Ahead of Game 3 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks, San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama was spotted enjoying NYC. A video has surfaced of Wemby sitting in a park in New York City, seemingly drawing a statue. It's hard to make out exactly what statue he was drawing, but he appeared razor-focused on it.

item.title: They’re favored to win according to both the …
item.description: Ahead of Game 3 of the NBA Finals tonight, the Knicks find themselves in a surreal position: They’re up 2-0 on the Spurs in the championship round, having already won more road Finals games in this series than they have in total since 1973, and now they’ll return to a Madison Square Garden crowd that is about to explode. (Or is that just fans’ credit scores?) They’re favored to win according to both the prediction markets (78 percent) and the stat models (86 percent), and not just because teams

item.title: Two deep-pocketed Big Apple firms — Gibson, Dunn & …
item.description: Madison Square Garden revealed the lucky winners of the coveted celebrity-row courtside seats for Game 3 of the NBA Finals — a giveaway that raked in a whopping $1 million for New York City’s youth. Two deep-pocketed Big Apple firms — Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LL and Veritas Capital — split the winning bid for the pair of hot-ticket seats as the New York Knicks return to the Garden Monday night for their first NBA Finals home showdown in more than 25 years against the San Antonio Spurs. The record

item.title: They understand that they've that it slipped through …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "After Game 1, the Spurs were very confident. They weren't beating their chest, but I think they were like, 'Yeah, we're not worried, we're fine.' The mood today was of missed opportunity. They understand that they've that it slipped through their fingers. Greg Popovich spoke to the team before they got on the plane, and he basically told them that. He's like, 'Don't think about one and two, don't think about the opportunities that you've had lost, think about the opportunity th

item.title: Fat Joe on James Dolan: Very misunderstood. They villainize him like Bruce Wayne in Gotham City
item.description: It wasn’t long before the cameras were pointing at Fat Joe, who shared his thoughts on the Knicks, who have a 2-0 lead over the Spurs and are just two wins away from winning their first title since 1973. He praised the Knicks for playing team basketball and exciting a city that hasn’t been to the Finals in 27 years. He also paid his respects to Knicks owner James Dolan. “Shoutout to Mr. Dolan, greatest team owner in the game,” Fat Joe said. “Very misunderstood. They villainize Mr. Dolan like alm

item.title: Cory Joseph and Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi …
item.description: Olympiacos point guard Cory Joseph and Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi Leonard reconnected over dinner in Athens, where the NBA star is currently spending his offseason vacation. The veteran players share a deep friendship that spans more than a decade, having both entered the league together as first-round picks in the 2011 NBA Draft, presented by Kia Motors. The Klaw was initially drafted 15th overall before being traded to the San Antonio Spurs, while Joseph joined him in Texas after being

item.title: “Realistically it’s probably Dodgeball, maybe Meet the …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs center Luke Kornet, on his favorites from Knicks super fan Ben Stiller: “Realistically it’s probably Dodgeball, maybe Meet the Parents, but I think my favorite character is in Heavyweights, which is kind of like a precursor to the Globo Gym. ... ‘Lunch has been canceled due to a lack of hustle’ is an all-timer.”

item.title: “I’m not sure it’s going to be a good reception for …
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakim Jeffries (D-N.Y.) in a new interview complained that President Trump is planning to attend Monday night’s Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs. “I’m not sure it’s going to be a good reception for him,” Jeffries said during the interview, which aired on CNN. “But why does Donald Trump always have to ruin a good thing, like literally the Knicks haven’t been in the finals for 27 years, the city is trying to c

item.title: De'Aaron Fox: 'The president being here just makes it inconvenient for everybody else'
item.description: Tom Orsborn: #Spurs guard De'Aaron Fox on security at MSG: “I think the president being here just makes it inconvenient for everybody else. ...We’re getting screened like it’s TSA. It’s a little inconvenient for the people that’s got to play, but it is what it is.”

item.title: Former Stanford teammate Anthony Goods: To paint a …
item.description: Former Stanford teammate Anthony Goods: To paint a picture: Mitch Johnson is a cool dude. I don’t mean he has a cool personality. I mean a laidback kind of cool, like how they used the term in the 1970s. Mitch walks slow. He talks slow. He even drives slow. When we were in college together at Stanford, we hung out all the time. If the speed limit was 65 miles per hour, I would be doing 80. Mitch would be going 62. That’s just Mitch. He knows who he is. And that’s why he’s a great leader.

item.title: Anthony Goods: Playing for Trent Johnson at Stanford …
item.description: Anthony Goods: Playing for Trent Johnson at Stanford wasn’t easy. He was a tough coach. Mitch would pick guys up. He went out of his way to make sure everybody was feeling good and that they had the energy they needed, all the way down to the walk-ons. We had a walk-on named Kenny Brown, and Mitch was always gassing him up in practice. He was everybody’s biggest cheerleader. He was the heart and soul of our team because he had relationships with everybody.

item.title: Madison Square Garden Sports — the James Dolan-run …
item.description: Madison Square Garden Sports — the James Dolan-run company that also owns the NHL’s New York Rangers — notched a record high of $390.12 a share on Thursday, the day after the Knicks clinched a 105-95 Game 1 victory over the San Antonio Spurs. Shares in MSGS soared 1.4% Monday to hit a fresh intraday high of $391.79, ahead of Game 3. The stock has rallied 103% over the last year, giving the sports conglomerate a $9.3 billion market cap.

item.title: Shams: President Donald Trump will not be sitting courtside at Game 3
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams: "President Donald Trump will be sitting in a suite tonight at Madison Square Garden so he's opting not to sit courtside"

item.title: "If we're desperate and we do what we're supposed to …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Spurs win game 3 if: "If we're desperate and we do what we're supposed to do" - Dylan Harper

item.title: The lines to get to Madison Square Garden are running …
item.description: Jared Weiss: The lines to get to Madison Square Garden are running blocks with all of the Security due to the Secret Service presence as president Donald Trump will be attending tonight’s NBA finals game three between the San Antonio Spurs and the New York Knicks.

item.title: Knicks tickets are still selling for thousands of …
item.description: Knicks tickets are still selling for thousands of dollars, but prices have plunged ahead of Game 3 at MSG. Increased security measures tied to U.S. President Donald Trump’s visit are a big reason why one of the hottest resale markets in sports is cooling down.

item.title: After a few chants of "USA, USA, USA" for the flag, …
item.description: After a few chants of "USA, USA, USA" for the flag, Trump was booed loudly when he was shown on the large video screens during the national anthem. The president was saluting and smiled slightly as the boos grew. Once the feed showed Knicks players, the cheering returned.

item.title: We have a big game tonight, and trying to continue to …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson, on De’Aaron Fox’s comments that the team has been inconvenienced by the president attending Game 3: “I have not felt any inconvenience. We have a big game tonight, and trying to continue to find a way to play closer to the standard and our identity than we have in the first two games. There’s a lot going on, and I’d much rather be part of it than not.”

item.title: I think he’s looking for what my job is, which is to …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson, on his conversations with Victor Wembanyama: “We definitely had some one-on-one conversations. I don't think he's looking for anything that he’s not throughout the season. I think he’s looking for what my job is, which is to help him, support him, and give him clarity.”

item.title: That doesn't mean that you're going to always get the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Got to ask Mitch Johnson about the positive vibes of his young Spurs team despite the difficult circumstances they’re in: “It’s the makeup of the group. I think when you single out a lot of the individuals in the group, and if you looked at their history and their basketball past and their personalities and how they are just wired, I guess it's no surprise. You still have to go do it. You have to go execute. That doesn't mean that you're going to always get the results you want, but

item.title: Mike Brown on expectations from Spurs: Their level of desperation is going to be heightened; if we don't match it, it will be tough
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on the Spurs in an 0-2 hole: "You know that your opponent, the level of desperation is going to be heightened, which means their physicality, how hard they play, is going to be heightened, and you know you have to try to match it or exceed it. If you don't, it could be, it could be tough."

item.title: So you have to make him work on on both ends of the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on Wemby: "You hope you can make him work... and you hope he misses some shots... then if you get a win, then you say, 'Thank goodness we were able to get out of there with a win,' because he's that impactful. So, I think it's a pretty simple formula: you're not blocking his shots, you're not getting them off his spots, you're not doing these things to him. him. So you have to make him work on on both ends of the floor and hope and pray that he misses some shots and we ca

item.title: My focus is just what's next and what's in front of me …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "I'm just locked in man on Game 3... My focus is just what's next and what's in front of me in Game 3 is in front of us right now." -Mike Brown when asked about Trump's trip to the Garden

item.title: "The term is a bunker mentality, it's actually …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown shouts out Popovich when asked about blocking out the noise: "The term is a bunker mentality, it's actually something that I learned from Pop back in the early 2000s when you get to this level, or you're playing in a bigger market, especially, there's going to be a lot of noise, and you're going to hit some adversity throughout the course of the season, and this is what I talked about when I said, you know, you hope you hit adversity, because you want to see how everybody

item.title: Mike Brown on foul discrepancy in Game 3: 'If they do this in Game 4, it's going to be tough for us to win'
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown: “Maybe we fouled 24 times but I’m baffled by (Spurs getting called for just 10 fouls in second half).” “If they do this in Game 4…it’s going to be tough for us to win,” Mike Brown says of foul discrepancy/uneven calls in Game 3.

item.title: He joins Hakeem Olajuwon and Patrick Ewing as the only …
item.description: ‪Justin Russo‬: Victor Wembanyama finished with 32 points, 8 rebounds, 6 assists, and 3 blocks in Game 3. He joins Hakeem Olajuwon and Patrick Ewing as the only players to do that in a postseason game at Madison Square Garden since blocks first became official in 1973-74.

item.title: First player since Shaq in 2001 to open the NBA Finals …
item.description: Tom Haberstroh: Victor Wembanyama in his first 3 NBA Finals Games 29.0 PPG 9.7 REB 3.3 BLK First player since Shaq in 2001 to open the NBA Finals Games 1-3 with at least those averages.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama with 32 points in the win, becoming …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Victor Wembanyama with 32 points in the win, becoming the third-youngest player to have 30+ points in the Finals in NBA history. Youngest since Magic Johnson in 1980.

item.title: Mitch Johnson on Wemby: "I'm sure Victor has numerous …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Mitch Johnson on Wemby: "I'm sure Victor has numerous sources of motivation, and I don't think any of us are surprised or expect anything different than a strong performance, and him being on his front foot in terms of being in attack mode"

item.title: We never told Victor, don't shoot an open three point …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch on Wemby getting to the basket: "Games take on different personalities and different opportunities can show themselves early, right. We never told Victor, don't shoot an open three point shot if you're open, even if it's early in the game or whatnot. But yeah, we wanted to put pressure on the paint, put pressure on the rim, and it can show itself in a lot of ways. And there's times where again he puts pressure on the rim, they tag him with one or two people, they open somethin

item.title: Wemby was asked about being a villain at MSG: “I …
item.description: Jared Weiss: Wemby was asked about being a villain at MSG: “I guess, I’m nowhere on Trae Young’s level though.”

item.title: “Less mistakes. More control.” - Wemby
item.description: Zena Keita‬: “Less mistakes. More control.” - Wemby Yall…Spurs might take Game 4. 👀

item.title: Jalen Brunson tonight: 3/9 in the paint
item.description: ‪Bobby Krivitsky‬: Jalen Brunson tonight: 3/9 in the paint He was great when he got all the way to the rim, but the Spurs did an excellent job of making life difficult for him when he tried to attack. No longer having Wemby guard KAT was a difference-maker.

item.title: Dylan Harper has scored 44 of the Spurs' 64 bench …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Dylan Harper has scored 44 of the Spurs' 64 bench points in the NBA Finals so far.

item.title: Only the second time in the Finals history the road …
item.description: ‪Dan Weiss: Only the second time in the Finals history the road team has won the first 3 games of the series. Spurs right back in this one! #PorVida

item.title: The team that has won the turnover battle has won all …
item.description: Law Murray: The team that has won the turnover battle has won all three games to begin the 2026 NBA Finals

item.title: Manu Ginobili: What a game! Very stressed out!
item.description: Manu Ginobili: What a game! Very stressed out! Way to grind it, @spurs!!

item.title: "These guys are tired. It's just what comes with it. I …
item.description: Myles Turner: "These guys are tired. It's just what comes with it. I don't understand what people think when I say tired. It's not just sleepy tired. It's not just like I'm a little fatigued. Mentally, emotionally, you're exhausted, bro. Like, side-to-side movement. People don't realize just, like, basketball is not just a one-dimensional sport where you're sprinting up and down the floor. Like, sliding laterally and what not, playing on different linear planes, that takes a lot out of you."

item.title: Spurs take Game 3 at MSG
item.description: James L. Edwards III‬: New York's 13-game playoff win streak ends with a Game 3 loss. Knicks fans will have to wait at least 48 hours to see their team win a Finals game at MSG. New York now has to travel to San Antonio at least one more time. 13 turnovers for 21 San Antonio points.

item.title: I’ve gotten used to it. I’ve only been traded one …
item.description: The NBA season could end this week in a way that it hasn’t in 53 years – with the New York Knicks as league champions. The Knicks won the first two games in the best-of-seven title series against the San Antonio Spurs, with Game 3 on Monday night. Former Auburn standout Isaac Okoro’s NBA season ended two months ago, and he expects this offseason to be much like the last few: He’ll be tuning out speculation about where he’ll be traded. “I think I’ll just control what I can control,” said Okoro, w

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on Stephon Castle: 'He might be the most mature player on our team'
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby on Steph Castle: "He might be the most mature player on our team, and he's nowhere near the oldest, and he's been in big games before. He's been in big games before the NBA. I'm not surprised by this, and he's shown over and over again that he's capable and that we are right to put our trust in him."

item.title: "Vic's probably number one. I don't go to sleep at …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Castle on Wemby's comment that Steph is the most mature player on the team: "Vic's probably number one. I don't go to sleep at 9pm, but I'm probably a close second."

item.title: “It’s just you and the rim.”
item.description: Jared Weiss: Steph Castle on his clutch free throws and the pressure he felt: “It’s just you and the rim.”

item.title: I think our confidence has been the same throughout …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Steph Castle: "Obviously it feels good to win, especially on the road after dropping two bad ones, but I think our confidence has been the same throughout this series, regardless of what happened. I feel like we're just looking forward to next 48."

item.title: His rating of his drawing: “Not bad. Pretty good.”
item.description: Jared Weiss: Wemby reveals what he drew in Gramercy Park. His rating of his drawing: “Not bad. Pretty good.”

item.title: "Really, tried to relax. The playoffs, is like a …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby confirms he drew the statue in the park, and spoke about clearing his mind: "Really, tried to relax. The playoffs, is like a whirlwind. Hard to put your head out the water, and sometimes it's like I don't even got to watch the game back right away, I just need like a little time off, let my brain cool down and recover, recover as much for the body as for the mind." Said the drawing was pretty good.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns: 'We didn't do what got us 13 straight wins in a row'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: KAT: "That ain't cost us the game. We turned the ball over, didn't execute, didn't do what got us 13 straight wins in a row. That's how you lose a game"

item.title: "Get to your spot, and if you miss that shot, like, is …
item.description: Tom Petrini: De'Aaron Fox his late shot: "Get to your spot, and if you miss that shot, like, is what it is. You try to make that shot more often than not, but it feels good to make, to hit a big shot down the stretch."

item.title: "We've guarded them pretty well... I forget what the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Fox on the scrambling defense: "We've guarded them pretty well... I forget what the stat was, but they were shooting a godly percentage under three seconds in the shot clock, and if you're playing 20, 21, 22 seconds of great defense, and they make shot, tap them on the butt, good job, but that's where we want to live, we want to live in that area of them trying to take tough shots."

item.title: Jalen Brunson on Wemby shoving him: "Whatever you saw …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen Brunson on Wemby shoving him: "Whatever you saw is what you saw"

item.title: "I shoot pressure free throws every day. Obviously, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon on his free throws late in the game: "I shoot pressure free throws every day. Obviously, you can't simulate anything like this. I step to the line in those moments, just me and the rim"

item.title: Victor Wembanyama has surpassed Dikembe Mutombo's 69 …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: 70 BLOCKS IN 20 POSTSEASON GAMES 👽 Victor Wembanyama has surpassed Dikembe Mutombo's 69 blocks during the 1994 postseason for the most blocks by a player in their first career playoff run since blocks were fully recorded.

item.title: Did you make eye contact? "Nah. I acted like I didn't …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Dylan Harper: "Before the game we came out and Jay Z just walked by me" SVP: Did you say what's up? "No" Did you make eye contact? "Nah. I acted like I didn't see him"

item.description: ALL NBA Podcast: Wemby's average shot distance and eFG% by game in the finals Game 1: 17.3 feet -- 33.3 eFG% Game 2: 13.2 feet -- 57.1 eFG% Game 3: 10.6 feet -- 66.7 eFG%

item.title: "It won't take that long, especially with my …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa is watching a young San Antonio Spurs team built by high draft picks in the NBA Finals and sees a similar chance to have quick success as a pro. That would be good news for the Washington Wizards, who have the No. 1 pick this year and are expected to use it on the star freshman from BYU. "It won't take that long, especially with my adaptability and my work ethic," Dybantsa said Monday. "I think that will be, I'm not going to say easy, but pretty similar to what these guys do in terms

item.title: Chris Finch on his Game 6 defensive changes: 'I kicked myself for doing it'
item.description: Chris Finch: "I felt that going into Game 6, we tried to make some adjustments that were clearly not right. I kicked myself for doing it, just because I felt we didn't probably need to be as extreme. I flipped the matchups around. We put Rudy Gobert on Castle, and we started with Julius Randle on Victor Wembanyama, trying to mitigate Wembanyama's hot starts. We did a good job, but then Castle got loose on us. It wasn't a scheme that we were overly comfortable with. We've done it from time to tim

item.title: Jalen Brunson debuted a new Nike Kobe 6 PE colorway …
item.description: colendri.com: Jalen Brunson debuted a new Nike Kobe 6 PE colorway against the San Antonio Spurs, finishing with 32 points, 5 rebounds, 5 assists in 35:27.

item.title: Rowdy Knicks fanatics clashed with cops outside Bryant …
item.description: Rowdy Knicks fanatics clashed with cops outside Bryant Park’s Game 3 watch party Monday night – including at least one who hurled a bottle at an officer. The sea of fans was already teetering over the edge just one hour after tip-off, as many didn’t make it inside the official watch park of the Knicks-Spurs game in the Midtown park. Huge brawls broke out and cops in riot gear rushed to control the unruly crowd, deploying pepper spray.

item.title: He recently changed representation agencies, …
item.description: Furthermore, it's likely that Real Madrid wouldn't be able to renew Lyles' contract even with a substantial offer. The player's main objective is to return to the NBA , where he already played 650 games over 10 seasons with the Jazz, Nuggets, Spurs, Pistons, and Kings . He recently changed representation agencies, specifically seeking a new chapter in the American league.

item.title: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team has lost each of the first 3 games of the series. The 1st such occurrence came in 1993, when the Chicago Bulls opened the Finals with two road wins over the Phoenix Suns before the Suns returned the favor in Game 3. (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: Victor Wembanyama has recorded at least 20 points, one …
item.description: ‪Justin Kubatko‬: Victor Wembanyama has recorded at least 20 points, one steal, and one block in each of his last 10 playoff games. It's the longest such postseason streak since the NBA began tracking steals and blocks in 1973-74. Read, share, subscribe ⬇️ open.substack.com/pub/statitud...

item.title: NBA to review Victor Wembanyama's first-quarter foul on Jalen Brunson
item.description: Marc Stein: The NBA, as is customary, will today review Victor Wembanyama’s first-quarter foul on Jalen Brunson to determine whether any additional penalties (such as a retroactive flagrant foul) are assessed.

item.title: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend Game …
item.description: Shams Charania: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend Game 4 of Knicks-Spurs in the NBA Finals on Wednesday night at Madison Square Garden due to scheduling conflicts and obligations, sources tell ESPN. Trump, a lifelong Knicks fan, watched the majority of Game 3 from a suite.

item.title: Hollywood stars and Wall Streeters pursuing Celebrity …
item.description: The hunt for Knicks tickets has created a cutthroat Hunger Games–like competition during this week’s NBA Finals. But Hollywood stars and Wall Streeters pursuing Celebrity Row seats at Madison Square Garden should be forewarned: There are some unofficial rules to scoring the ultimate in luxury, courtside seats, say sources. As the Knicks and Spurs face off at the World’s Most Famous Arena, they’ll be flanked by a few dozen camera-friendly seats that have become known for hosting A-listers like Ti

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: The best shot in the game is an alley-oop, but teams don't just let you do that
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Victor Wembanyama on his increased activity inside in Game 3: "The goal is always to go inside. The best shot in the game is an alley-oop. The most efficient shot. But teams don't just let you do that."

item.title: Informed of videos shared on social media showing fans …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama was among players on both the Spurs and Knicks to condemn the videos permeating social media of apparent New York fans physically attacking San Antonio supporters. Informed of videos shared on social media showing fans attacking Spurs fans on the streets and ripping off their jerseys, Wembanyama denounced those actions. "My thoughts of course [are] that we can't forget it's a game," he said. "We're just playing a game out there. I am all for passion, but [with] the respect of e

item.title: "The game is built off of respect and passion," Knicks …
item.description: "The game is built off of respect and passion," Knicks center Karl-Anthony Towns said. "We want everyone to respect each other. We want everyone to enjoy basketball at its purest state. It's the NBA Finals. There's no better place to watch basketball. Leave the physicality to everyone on the court" A Brooklyn native, Spurs forward Julian Champagnie pointed out that he doesn't "feel any threat" walking around Midtown Manhattan during San Antonio's time off the court.

item.title: Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: NYC native Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden: "The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing all the celebrities, the fans, how loud it was, the music, it was definitely a surreal feeling that I'll carry for life. ... It was pretty cool."

item.title: LeBron James on Spurs: 'They're a team that doesn't have ego'
item.description: LeBron James: "San Antonio, they're a team that doesn't have ego. They have their personal beliefs on what they can bring to the table. We all do, we're all competitors, but that team is egoless. It's big time to see Josh (Hart) kind of growing to the player that he's in. We always talk about Alex Caruso a lot. It's those certain guys that you can plug into any franchise, any team, and they're going to win because those guys just make winning plays."

item.title: It seemed like Brunson was more so worried about the …
item.description: DeMarcus Cousins: "Last night, I didn't like it. I feel like they switched up their style of play as far as allowing the offense to flow through KAT. It seemed like Brunson was more so worried about the headline, worried about being maybe the Finals MVP. That's what I saw in the moment. It didn't seem like it was the right form of basketball last night and I felt it was a lot of forced offense."

item.title: I’m so proud of him for the strength that he has shown …
item.description: Maria credits her culture with shaping her approach to parenting. She saw her oldest child Ron Jr. struggling under the pressure of his namesake. She had to find the balance between protecting him and allowing him to go through those life experiences on his own. "I like to call him my crockpot," she said. "It’s a slow simmer but what comes out is really delicious. I’m so proud of him for the strength that he has shown and proud of myself too for allowing him to go through those moments even thou

item.title: NBA rules no additional calls for Victor Wembanyama's shove on Jalen Brunson
item.description: Marc Stein: The NBA says there will be no additional sanctions against San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama for his first-quarter shove of Jalen Brunson in Game 3. The play is not being upgraded to a flagrant foul ... meaning Wembanyama remains on two flagrant foul points this postseason.

item.title: We’re seeing three different areas of KAT that had …
item.description: Towns has been dominant on both ends of the court against the Spurs, often getting the better of Victor Wembanyama, who’s considered the next face of the league. “It’s huge because, one, he’s rising to the occasion,” Lin said. “Two, he’s doing it on the defensive end as well. Three, he has turned up his playmaking abilities. We’re seeing three different areas of KAT that had certain narratives already written about him, so credit to him. And from all of his interviews, he seems so centered, so g

item.title: We'll win this series on the court (even if the refs …
item.description: Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Couldn't agree more. We'll win this series on the court (even if the refs refuse to call a flagrant on Wemby), not by targeting, harassing, or attacking Spurs fans. Knicks in 5.

item.title: Mike Brown on Karl-Anthony Towns' lack of touches: I got to do a better job of getting him more involved
item.description: So it put Towns is back into focus. "It's extremely important that he's getting touches, that he's involved, not just in the fourth quarter but obviously throughout the ballgame," Knicks coach Mike Brown said Tuesday at practice. "I've got to continue trying to do a better job of getting him involved throughout the course of the game, as well as late."

item.title: "Everybody is disappointed that we didn't go out and …
item.description: "We have a veteran group. Nobody is quote-unquote 'panicking' or anything like that," Brown said. "Everybody is disappointed that we didn't go out and execute, and play to what we feel our standard is. That's not taking anything away from San Antonio, but we feel like we can play a lot better than what we did."

item.title: The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing …
item.description: During the Spurs media availability on Tuesday afternoon, he was asked, “After Game 7 in OKC, you were asked about returning back to New York. Now in the Finals, now that you’ve experienced that, could you share what it was like for you to play last night?” “Yeah, it was pretty cool. Obviously we had a game to win, and that was the most important thing, especially being down 0-2. The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing all the celebrities, the fans, how loud it was, the music, it

item.title: “I think we contributed to the confusion by not being …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs attempted to make a substitution with just over five minutes left in Game 3 at Madison Square Garden in New York, N.Y. Initially, the substitution did not take place, but officials abruptly whistled the play dead just after the New York Knicks had inbounded the ball to try to figure out what was going on. NBA head of officiating Monty McCutchen felt that the game officials were not authoritative enough and got themselves into a mess as a result. “You have to run the game wi

item.title: Attendance…The actions of the Mayor and Police …
item.description: Ian Begley: Statement from MSG Sports: “We have been told that the NYPD will once again implement the same 'frozen zone' restrictions for Game 4, which were supposedly to thwart any threats related to the President's attendance…The actions of the Mayor and Police Commissioner, supposedly in support of the Knicks and their fans, are difficult to understand.” Full statement below. Police previously said prior to Game 3 that the area around MSG was shut down due to President’s visit. This action wo

item.title: The New Yorks Knicks 105-104 victory over the San …
item.description: The New Yorks Knicks 105-104 victory over the San Antonio Spurs on June 5 was ABC’s most-watched NBA Finals Game 2 since 2018 – according to Nielsen Big Data + Panel. Game 2 generated an average audience of 16.43M viewers and peaked with 19.42M viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. Viewership was up 88% from last year’s Game 2.

item.title: Those first 2 games really hurt, and I feel like it …
item.description: Ballislife.com: Stephon Castle on if he's expecting to win Game 4 at MSG: "Yeah, definitely. I expect us to win the next three, but we're just taking it game by game, quarter by quarter, and trying to walk this thing down. Those first 2 games really hurt, and I feel like it was an eye-opener for us. We don't want it to be that way. We want to punch first."

item.title: Spurs have clean injury report ahead of Game 4
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs have clean injury report ahead of Game 4.

item.title: Jose Alvarado on Victor Wembanyama tossing Jalen Brunson to floor: That's something that they gotta look at, that'll be the last one
item.description: In the first quarter of the Spurs’ victory at the Garden, Wembanyama tossed Jalen Brunson to the floor. No foul was called. “I think that’s not basketball,” Jose Alvarado, the feisty reserve guard from Brooklyn, said on Tuesday. “That’s something that they gotta look at. But he got away with one. That’ll be the last one.”

item.title: Stephon Castle on Game 4: 'I expect us to win the next three'
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Stephon Castle on if he expects to win game 4 in Madison Square Garden “I expect us to win the next three”

item.title: Rick Brunson on defending Jalen Brunson during scuffle with De'Aaron Fox: 'I'm always gonna be a father first'
item.description: “I’m always gonna be a father first,” Rick said on “Good Morning America” Monday during a joint interview with Jalen. “But at the end of the day, if there was any other player, I’d do the same.” For Rick, the flashpoint was just another reminder of the emotional volatility that comes with watching his son operate under the brightest lights in basketball — something he says still feels is surreal. “It’s crazy to me. I never envisioned, you know, to this level,” he said of Jalen’s rise.

item.title: If there's a clear miss, then there's a possibility …
item.description: Ian Begley: In the NBA Today interview, McCutchen acknowledged that Wembanyama committed a foul and explained the standard involved in elevating a call: “Every game is reviewed by the league office postgame, the entirety of the game, and we're in the process of doing that today. Most certainly, it's important to note, though, as we do that review, that there's a real deference, just like in our replay center, Malika, that we want a clear and conclusive standard to upgrade something, because we d

item.title: Madison Square Garden and the New York Knicks are mad …
item.description: Madison Square Garden and the New York Knicks are mad at the New York Police Department and Mayor Zohran Mamdani for not letting them throw a big enough party for Game 4 of the NBA Finals. In a statement released Tuesday, the team called NYPD commissioner Jessica Tisch and Mamdani the city’s “biggest party poopers” for putting security restrictions around the Garden for Wednesday night’s contest against the San Antonio Spurs. While the city has approved a watch party, the venue is complaining th

item.title: "I didn’t know that… We’re just playing a game out …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama when asked about reports of Knicks fans attacking Spurs fans in New York after Game 3: "I didn’t know that… We’re just playing a game out there. And I’m all for passion, but to the respect of each other. It’s unacceptable."

item.title: “He pushed him down and none of the guys on the Knicks …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Draymond Green said if Wemby did to Steph what he did to Brunson in Game 3…he would’ve been thrown out. “He pushed him down and none of the guys on the Knicks team did anything. Don’t not for one second think that didn’t matter.”

item.title: Victor Wembanyama could be the youngest player to lead the NBA playoffs in scoring
item.description: Jorge Sierra: As of now, Victor Wembanyama would replace LeBron James as the youngest player in NBA history to lead the playoffs in scoring. Only has 23 points more than Jalen Brunson, though. Also: LeBron would still be No. 1 if the series goes to Game 7.

item.title: He shoots me a call or shoots me a text, usually me, …
item.description: Carter Bryant: “Vic loves to do extracurricular activities. He shoots me a call or shoots me a text, usually me, him and HB. ... We’ve done painting, we’ve done game days like playing board games. We went to the world’s quietest room. We’ve done a bunch of dope stuff.”

item.title: “Yeah, we’ll have to figure it out,” Karl-Anthony …
item.description: They’ve fallen to early first-quarter deficits in each of the three games of this series so far. In their Game 3 loss Monday, they trailed by as many as 12 points in the first quarter. In Games 1 and 2, they trailed by as many as 10 in the first quarter. “Yeah, we’ll have to figure it out,” Karl-Anthony Towns said Tuesday. “We’ll have to figure it out because in every game we’ve played here in the Finals, we’ve put ourselves in a position where we have to start fighting immediately. Every time w

item.title: Wembanyama didn’t come flying out of the paint to …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama didn’t come flying out of the paint to close out on a shooter at the top of the key, leaving him able to track Shamet when the ball came to him in the corner. That’s by design. “At this point,” Fox said, “when you’ve played a team so many times, you know what they’re going to do. They know what you’re going to do. They got us in rotations and … teams want to get Vic out of the paint. You know that’s not a secret. We have gotten good about rotating around him, and he’s gotten be

item.title: “I don’t think I’ve seen a player at his age handle …
item.description: “I don’t think I’ve seen a player at his age handle the platform and pressure that he’s been able to, with the grace that he’s been able to,” said veteran Spurs forward Harrison Barnes, who won a ring with Curry in Golden State in 2015 and played alongside Dirk Nowitzki in Dallas. “He feels very natural in those moments,” Barnes said. “I think, coming into (Monday) night, in 14 years, that was one of the craziest atmospheres I’ve seen for a playoff game. Especially a finals game. I feel like he

item.title: In Games 1 and 2, he had trouble getting and …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama struggled when he was guarded by Towns in the first two games, shooting just 7-for-20 with Towns as his primary defender. On Monday, he was 6-for-10 for 14 points. In Games 1 and 2, he had trouble getting and converting lobs as the Knicks pushed him away from the basket. In Game 3, he was 4-for-4 on lob plays, according to Genius IQ. After the game, Wembanyama was asked about his public outing in New York City and his stop at Gramercy Park. He didn't remember the name of the pa

item.title: He has a habit of visiting with his two agents, Bouna …
item.description: As Victor Wembanyama has often said, he is a young man who greatly values his routine. To that end, he has a habit of visiting with his two agents, Bouna Ndiaye and Jeremy Medjana, immediately after every postgame press conference.

item.title: Per league sources, Rockets coach and former Spurs …
item.description: Per league sources, Rockets coach and former Spurs assistant Ime Udoka was a huge fan of Stephon Castle’s (which Castle said he was well aware of). But with veteran Fred VanVleet manning the point guard position and young Jalen Green at the two-guard spot, Castle and his camp sent the kinds of signals Houston’s way that eventually led to him landing in San Antonio. Just as he’d hoped. “I think being (in San Antonio) was always number one on my list,” said Castle, who won Rookie of the Year in 20

item.title: The latest is located in New York Midtown, between …
item.description: Kaysuane has put together two murals during the Knicks’ spectacular run through the playoffs. The latest is located in New York Midtown, between 30th Street and 31st Street and 8th Avenue, depicting Jalen Brunson as the “King of the East,” with NBA guards Trae Young, Tyrese Maxey and James Harden bowing to the throne. Next to the image is a Larry O’Brien championship trophy with the Knicks logo drawn over it. A Game 3 loss in the NBA Finals hasn’t taken the spirit from Kaysuane or the New York C

item.title: Stephon Castle, going through shootaround drills, …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Stephon Castle, going through shootaround drills, tipped his hat yesterday to Mitch and his staff for their role in the big Game 3 win: “Our coaches do a good job of explaining why we lost … and what we can do to help ourselves.”

item.title: A source tells us, “She’s a huge fan of the Knicks and …
item.description: Is this Taylor Swift’s ultimate makeup song for the Knicks? Page Six can exclusively confirm that Taylor Swift will be attending Wednesday’s Game 4 of the NBA Finals, in which the New York Knicks will be taking on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden. A source tells us, “She’s a huge fan of the Knicks and really wants to be there to support them. She is going with friends.”

item.title: Luke Kornet on Derrick White: I don’t know if we’re exactly on speaking terms
item.description: Q. Have you heard from any of the Boston guys during the run here? And I know Derrick White kind of talked to you a little bit about what to expect in San Antonio and making that decision. Like what has it been like to have a guy like that in your corner as you've made this transition? And have you heard from anybody else through this run here? Luke Kornet: Yeah, I heard from guys, I've been in touch with them. There's a group of us that are still like kind of texting throughout the year and wha

item.title: “Naw, that ain’t cost us the game,” Towns said. …
item.description: Though Brown felt the free throw issue hurt his team, Towns did not. Or, at least he did not say so publicly. “Naw, that ain’t cost us the game,” Towns said. “Turned the ball over. Didn’t execut. Didn’t do what got us 13 straight wins in a row. That’s how you lose a game. We didn’t do what we did for 13; we decided to do something different. … It’s a clear indication of how you’re going to lose the game, especially in the playoffs.”

item.title: “So, I’m a Governor of the NBA, right, I’m restricted …
item.description: Dolan was referring to the NBA’s decision not to retroactively assess a flagrant foul on Victor Wembanyama for tossing Jalen Brunson to the ground in the first quarter of Game 3. There was no foul called on the play either. “So, I’m a Governor of the NBA, right, I’m restricted and bound by the rules of the NBA, of which there are a lot. There’s little that I can say,” Dolan said. “I will say if you read the cover of The New York Post today, I think they got it right. Hopefully, that won’t get me

item.title: The games are shown in barbershops, laundromats, and …
item.description: In Fort Greene, Brooklyn, a towering broadcast was projected on the side of a building as people watched from the street and on nearby rooftops. Outside the Sugar Hill Creamery in Harlem, kids devoured ice cream as the game was illuminated on pieces of poster board tied to scaffolding. Inmates at Rikers Island with records of good behavior were granted a viewing. The games are shown in barbershops, laundromats, and at least one gas station in the West Village. And any bar, bodega or pizzeria wit

item.title: Throughout the postseason—which began for the Knicks …
item.description: As the Knicks vie for their first NBA title since 1973, law and finance professionals tell Front Office Sports that the historic playoff run has provided some rare moments of relief from New York’s famously relentless work culture. Throughout the postseason—which began for the Knicks on April 18—sources say their inboxes have gotten leaner and some requests have less urgency. The change isn’t because business has slowed; it’s because the executives, partners, and clients who dictate the pace of

item.title: A spokesperson for Delmonico’s, a fine-dining …
item.description: A spokesperson for Delmonico’s, a fine-dining restaurant in the Financial District, tells FOS it has seen a marked increase in demand for private dining rooms over the last month—often from corporate groups looking to watch games together.

item.title: Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals between the San Antonio …
item.description: NBA Communications: Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks was the most-watched NBA Finals Game 3 in 28 years (since Bulls/Jazz in 1998), averaging 23.8 million viewers on ABC and ESPN. The audience peaked with 26.3 million viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. Game 4 tips off tonight at 8:30 p.m. ET on ABC.

item.title: 76 points ties for 3rd-most in a Finals 1st half
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Spurs ... -- 76 points ties for 3rd-most in a Finals 1st half -- 27-point lead ties for 3rd-biggest at a Finals halftime 76-49 at the break.

item.title: Spurs scored 76 points in 48 possessions in the first …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Spurs scored 76 points in 48 possessions in the first half. On pace to have a 158.3 offensive rating on the road in the NBA Finals.

item.title: 12 years ago to the day, the Spurs shot 75.8% in the …
item.description: Dan Weiss: 12 years ago to the day, the Spurs shot 75.8% in the first half & scored 71 points in Miami on their way to a rout of the Heat. Tonight, they shoot 60% in the first half & make an NBA Finals record 14 threes in a half and score 76 points to lead by 27. #PorVida

item.title: "This guy could be he could be an a**hole if he wanted …
item.description: NBA Base: De’Aaron Fox on Victor Wembanyama: "This guy could be he could be an a**hole if he wanted it to be because of the talent that he has at such a young age. And he’s nowhere near that and he’s nowhere near his potential as well. So, I mean, he’s a better person than he is basketball player. And I think that’s saying something because we know we all know how good he is as a basketball player. And he’s just selfless."

item.title: "I thought he played with really good energy. I …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch on Keldon in Game 3: "I thought he played with really good energy. I thought he tried to rebound himself into the game and play good team defense, and that's what we need from him. We know he can score the basketball, and that will come with just minutes and opportunity, but his ability to rebound for us on both ends and be a strong team defender is what we need him to do."

item.title: When you start peeling back the layers, the toughness …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch Johnson on getting to know Steph Castle: "A lot of times I think as coaches we ignorantly get a player and then it's like their book starts and there's so much history that you can learn about a player and a person before they get to you, and so when you look back at his time, whether it's being coached by Coach Hurley, whether that's how he's raised in his household by his mother and father, whether that's him playing off the ball and sacrificing maybe some of the on-ball opp

item.title: He's played on the ball, he's played off the ball, …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch on Steph Castle: "He's found his rhythm because we've asked him to do every single thing that a player can be asked since he's been drafted. I know that goes back two years, not just this year, but he started, he's been brought off the bench. He's played on the ball, he's played off the ball, he's guarded primary ball creators, he's guarded bigger wings, and even some quote unquote bigger players or big guys. He's fearless, he's relentless. He probably doesn't get enough credi

item.title: Early on, I think we started out 2-3, and everybody …
item.description: Kris Pursiainen: Mike Brown on what moment made him realize this Knicks group was so resilient: "Lot of 'em. Early on, I think we started out 2-3, and everybody thought the wheels were coming off the bus. It could've easily been that...we could've had guys go their separate ways at that time...we just kept taking it one day at at a time...we're sitting here now."

item.title: Mike Brown on OG Anunoby this series: 'I think he can reach an even higher plateau'
item.description: Kris Pursiainen: Mike Brown on OG Anunoby against the Spurs, thus far: "He's getting better as the series has gone along. He's been great for us in the playoffs so far. I think he can reach an even higher plateau"

item.title: "The league's gonna do what they're gonna do, ain't …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on the NBA decision to not call a retroactive flagrant on Wemby: "The league's gonna do what they're gonna do, ain't gonna listen to me, they ain't gonna listen to nobody else, and I said my piece on what I said after the game two days ago, and you just hope that at the end of the day everything's consistent on both ends throughout the whole game."

item.title: I gotta do a better job of trying ot find ways to get …
item.description: Kris Pursiainen: Mike Brown says foul bonus affects Mitch Robinson's minutes: "I did limit Mitch in Game 3, when we were in the bonus. I gotta do a better job of trying ot find ways to get him on the floor. And it's not to "give KAT a break," I gotta do a better job of getting him touches in the fourth."

item.description: Marc Stein: Charles Barkley on the Spurs' collapse from 29 points up: "We saw the dumbest basketball team in the history of civilization."

item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Knicks came back from down 29 & won, the largest comeback win in NBA Finals history.

item.description: ‪James L. Edwards III‬: The Knicks, known for their resiliency, became the first team in NBA Finals history to win after trailing by 22-plus-points at halftime. New York is one win away from the NBA title.

item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Spurs in the first half tonight: 158.3 offensive rating, 74.5 eFG%. In the second half: 66.7 offensive rating, 24.4 eFG%

item.title: This was the Knicks’ second great comeback of the …
item.description: This was the Knicks’ second great comeback of the playoffs; they also fought from 22 down with eight minutes left in Game 1 of the conference finals to beat Cleveland in overtime. New York can close out the finals and claim the franchise’s third title with a win Saturday in San Antonio. Game 5 is at 8:30 p.m. at the Frost Bank Center.

item.title: OG Anunoby's putback caps stunning 29-point comeback as Knicks take 3-1 series lead
item.description: NBA: OG ANUNOBY WITH THE PUTBACK. KNICKS COMPLETE THE 29-PT COMEBACK FOR THE WIN. LARGEST COMEBACK IN NBA FINALS HISTORY

item.title: Knicks come back from 20 in the fourth quarter, the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: NBA teams were 4-750 when down by 20 or more in the 4th quarter of a playoff game over the last 30 years. They are now 5-750. Knicks come back from 20 in the fourth quarter, the second time they've come back from down 20 in the fourth THIS POSTSEASON.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: 'We've proven that we can surpass these difficulties'
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby on how the Spurs move on: "Holding each other accountable, communicating, not pointing fingers, and after that we either got it or don't, but we've proven that we can surpass these difficulties, and even though we haven't been there before, I'm believing that we're built this way, and we're gonna use the better of this, and tighten us up."

item.title: Wemby: "I think it's going one of two ways. The bad …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby: "I think it's going one of two ways. The bad one would be giving up. The good one would be getting stronger from this, getting more together"

item.title: "I told them to feel the emotion tonight, because …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch on how the Spurs even begin to bounce back from this: "I told them to feel the emotion tonight, because they're competitive, and that's not humanly possible to not take all the emotion out. They're going to need to be ready to play in two days."

item.title: De'Aaron Fox: 'I tried to get a layup to get up three'
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs’ De’Aaron Fox on his late layup attempt: “I tried to get a layup to get up three. Force them to need a three, and OG [Anunoby] made a good block.”

item.title: “I just thought I’d be able to outrun [Knicks’ OG …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs’ De’Aaron Fox on attempting the layup late rather than running the clock: “I just thought I’d be able to outrun [Knicks’ OG Anunoby]. That’s it.”

item.title: Stephon Castle: “Coach Mitch said it best, we’ve …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs’ Stephon Castle: “Coach Mitch said it best, we’ve pretty much dictated the winner and loser of all these games.”

item.description: StatMuse: Dylan Harper in Game 4: 21 PTS 8-12 FG +12 The youngest 20 PT game in Finals history.

item.title: "Even though we haven't been there before, I'm …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "Even though we haven't been there before, I'm convinced we are built this way." Wemby on how the Spurs will recover from blowing a 29-point lead.

item.title: Most tickets for Game 5 in San Antonio are being …
item.description: NBA Central: Most tickets for Game 5 in San Antonio are being purchased by New Yorkers, with 37% of sales coming from New York. The get-in price has also surged by $1,000 following the Knicks’ Game 4 win Top billing states: • New York — 37% • Texas — 12% • New Jersey — 8% (Ticket prices via @TickPick )

item.title: "You're a scumbag, your mom and dad are scumbags, and …
item.description: Charles Barkley on Knicks fans who attacked Spurs fan- "You're a scumbag, your mom and dad are scumbags, and your kids are scumbags."

item.title: -The Spurs need a heartbreak to prep for their …
item.description: Michael Redd: Knicks pull off a Game 4 W for the ages. My takeaways: -The Spurs need a heartbreak to prep for their eventual Championship, and tonight was it. Every great team has gone through this. They'll cry in the locker room tonight and be better for it in the coming seasons -I never felt SAS' lead was safe. The Knicks are the more mature team, they're physical, and they smell blood when it's in the water. You can't let a team like that hang around. You gotta kill them off fully -Experience
