item.title: Spurs set NBA Finals record with largest halftime lead in histroy
item.description: Justin Russo: The 27-point lead by the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 is the largest halftime lead by a road team in the history of the NBA Finals, excluding the bubble.

item.title: At Game 4 of the NBA Finals between the New York …
item.description: Even though the Knicks won, you can say Taylor Swift spurred media buzz with her courtside look. At Game 4 of the NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden on June 10, Taylor Swift showed up in a custom blue-and-orange “Stevie Knicks” T-shirt, a playful mashup of the Knicks and legendary singer Stevie Nicks. The shirt immediately caught the attention of fans online, especially after photos and videos of Swift courtside began circulating across soci

item.title: “Come on, it’s the Knicks, baby!” Chalamet yelled …
item.description: Actor Timothée Chalamet declared “Knicks in 5” after his hometown team pulled off a historic comeback to beat the Spurs 107-106 in Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday night. “Come on, it’s the Knicks, baby!” Chalamet yelled while on the court at Madison Square Garden, as seen in a video online after the Knicks took a 3-1 series lead. He held up five fingers before bringing his hands to a prayer gesture.

item.title: For longtime Knicks faithful, the historic win was …
item.description: Madison Square Garden remained packed after Game 4 ended with hometown fanatics too thrilled to leave the arena, clips showed. “It was the largest comeback ever. If any team could do it, the New York Knicks can. They’re going to win the championship,” said Tommy Connors, 19, of Queens, a few blocks up from the Garden. “Wemby [San Antonio villain Victory Wembanyama] folded under pressure. He couldn’t handle the Garden. He’s crying right now.” For longtime Knicks faithful, the historic win was eve

item.title: Then, in the very next moment, as if it were the most …
item.description: One night later, Stacey ripped his suit pants from the thigh to the ankle while boarding the bus to the arena. He gently chided our producer for wanting to use gaffer tape to fix a very expensive outfit. But he kept his infectious smile as our producer stapled his pants shut. When we sat down at our broadcast position, he pointed across the floor at a then-19-year-old Victor Wembanyama and said, “Just watch. That dude is about to change the league.” Then, in the very next moment, as if it were t

item.title: Victor Wembanyama closer to suspension after second flagrant foul
item.description: San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama moved one step closer to an automatic suspension after logging his second flagrant foul of the postseason in the third quarter of Wednesday night's 107-106 Game 4 NBA Finals loss to the New York Knicks. The Knicks lead the series 3-1. Having now collected three flagrant foul points this postseason, Wembanyama is only one point away from an automatic suspension. He committed the latest transgression, a flagrant foul 1, with 9:27 left in the third quarter o

item.title: “Can’t really explain it right now,” he said bluntly. …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama, 22, also missed a pair of free throws late in the game with the Spurs clinging to a one-point lead with a little less than two minutes on the clock. “Can’t really explain it right now,” he said bluntly. “It’s just execution. Greediness, of some sort. We clearly weren’t the hungriest in the second half.”

item.title: “We’ve got to try to put it behind us,” De'Aaron Fox …
item.description: The chosen plan led to pain, yet again, and the Spurs now find themselves on the edge of much offseason regret. “We’ve got to try to put it behind us,” De'Aaron Fox said. “Get back to the things that we’ve done well in these games. … We have to figure out a way to hold the lead. We’ve been able to build double-digit leads in all four of these games, and we’ve got to figure out a way to sustain that. “It obviously looks like a steep hill, but this is something that’s happened before. … We feel li

item.title: Added Spurs rookie Dylan Harper: “It’s hit me, but …
item.description: Added Spurs rookie Dylan Harper: “It’s hit me, but probably (I’m in) shock.” “That’s a game where you sit there and you say you had the type of personnel that you had, you shot the ball decent, played a pretty clean game,” San Antonio coach Mitch Johnson said. “Then kind of didn’t finish the job.”

item.title: "I feel like even up to the last buzzer, we believed …
item.description: "I feel like even up to the last buzzer, we believed we were going to win or that we were supposed to win," said NBA Sixth Man of the Year Keldon Johnson. "We're all human. It hurts. We want to win. We're so close, but so far. We're going to continue what we do, bring our brand of basketball and correct the things we can correct going forward. I believe, one through 15, whoever steps on the court, we're going out there to get the job done."

item.title: Spurs should still start De'Aaron Fox over Dylan …
item.description: Run It Back: Despite Game 4, Spurs should still start De'Aaron Fox over Dylan Harper in the series. "You have to stick with him at this point." - Boogie Cousins "Pulling him now... is not the right decision." - @ChandlerParsons

item.title: De’Aaron Fox made a huge mistake when he got the ball …
item.description: Earvin Magic Johnson: We just watched the Knicks make the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history! The first and second half were two completely different stories - the Spurs dominated in the first half and Knicks stormed back in the second. Jalen Brunson put the team on his back with his 36-point performance, but OG Anunoby was the true hero tonight, finishing with 33 points AND the game-winning tip in! De’Aaron Fox made a huge mistake when he got the ball with seconds left in the fourth and de

item.title: Victor Wembanyama nearly hit by egg after Game 4
item.description: A brutal night for Victor Wembanyama continued even after he returned to his hotel on Wednesday, as he was pelted with boos from jeering Knicks fans and nearly struck by a flying egg. A video shared online showed at least one egg tossed in the direction of the San Antonio Spurs superstar as he entered his hotel, flanked by security, following the team's game 4 loss to the Knicks. It was not clear who threw the egg. The video showed taunting fans swarming the hotel, a few blocks south of Madison

item.title: You're throwing eggs at Victor Wembanyama... if you're …
item.description: ClutchPoints: “If you're throwing eggs at Victor Wembanyama... if you're beating up people... wearing Spurs jerseys, just know that you are a disgrace. You're not disgracing the city, you're disgracing yourself." Mike Greenberg RIPPED Knicks fans on @GetUpESPN .

item.title: The New York Police Department arrested 15 people and …
item.description: The New York Police Department arrested 15 people and released another 41 with criminal court summonses Wednesday night, as crowds gathered outside Madison Square Garden during and after the New York Knicks’ comeback win over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 of the NBA Finals. Objects that appeared to be eggs were also thrown at Spurs star Victor Wembanyama as he walked from the team bus into the team hotel following the game, according to multiple videos on social media. The NYPD estimated that

item.title: Tom Crean: This was coming. I watched Game 4 of the …
item.description: Tom Crean was the head coach at Indiana, where he coached OG Anunoby for two years. He is now an NBA and college basketball analyst. Tom Crean: This was coming. I watched Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday night from a hotel room. Early on, I could see OG Anunoby’s confidence. I’ve known him since he was 16. He was ready. In the closing seconds of the game, after he blocked a shot on the other end, he took the ball out of bounds to inbound it for the final play. I saw the way the San Antonio

item.title: The problem for Towns is that Game 3 — and much of …
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns comes into Game 5 averaging 16.7 points, 10.6 rebounds and 5.2 assists during the postseason. He was the aggressor over the first two games, both Knicks wins, in his matchup against 22-year-old San Antonio Spurs’ sensation Victor Wembanyama. As Wiggins did during Golden State’s title run, Towns has lifted the level of his own game at various points in the postseason. The problem for Towns is that Game 3 — and much of Game 4 — reinforced the labels that have followed him throug

item.title: Iman Shumpert: 'De'Aaron Fox will kick himself for a while for that fourth quarter'
item.description: Iman Shumpert: Now, what I will say is me being a team an old teammate of De'Aaron Fox, De'Aaron Fox will kick himself for a while for that fourth quarter. I I'm not sure what happened. I don't mind all game for what everything that was going on. A lot of these plays, De'Aaron Fox knows better. The lackadaisical turnovers, especially the live ball turnovers that led to breakaways. The indecision, the step back threes that he never shoots and never settles for, but he just decided to do it yester

item.title: Shumpert was set to appear on “The Hoop Collective” …
item.description: Iman Shumpert had postgame commitments after the Knicks’ wild Game 4 win. He decided to make other plans. The former Knicks guard bailed on an ESPN podcast appearance Wednesday night so he could join the celebration outside Madison Square Garden after the Knicks’ dramatic NBA Finals comeback win over the San Antonio Spurs. Shumpert was set to appear on “The Hoop Collective” podcast but said he instead was heading to Seventh Avenue with J.R. Smith to celebrate with the fans outside MSG.

item.title: “That was just probably the dumbest, ... most arrogant …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “That was just probably the dumbest, ... most arrogant game that's ever been played." @realchriswebber Chris Webber reacts to the Spurs blowing the biggest lead in NBA Finals history.

item.title: "I can confirm that multiple eggs were thrown in the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams: "I can confirm that multiple eggs were thrown in the direction of Victor Wembanyama late last night outside the Spurs hotel. As of right now the New York Police Department has not made any arrests. Their hotel for games 3 and 4 was located just about a 5 block radius from Madison Square Garden"

item.title: The NBA’s two-minute report uncovers two officiating …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: The NBA’s two-minute report uncovers two officiating mistakes, and neither involved OG Anunoby’s block on Fox. The first was Wemby should’ve been whistled for a defensive 3-second violation with 1:26 left. The second was Josh Hart should’ve been whistled for a foul on Stephon Castle with 1:03 left. On OG’s block on Fox, the NBA determined, “Anunoby makes a legal attempt to block Fox's shot and dislodges the ball from his control before making incidental arm contact.”

item.title: Bontemps: What will LeBron James do? For the first …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: What will LeBron James do? For the first time since James came to Los Angeles eight years ago, there's a legitimate chance he won't be playing for the Lakers next season, either because of retirement or because he has chosen to close out his career elsewhere. The door isn't shut on his return to the Purple and Gold, but it does mean it's a far more interesting summer than usual for James. That's partly because it's unclear what James and the Lakers want to do. Los Angeles wants to

item.title: James Dolan on Spurs: We had Cardi B and Wu-Tang Clan, they had a dog on a beach ball
item.description: James Dolan has been the Knicks' owner since the late-1990s, finally enjoying his second NBA Finals appearance and first since 1999. With a title getting closer to his grasp, the owner went on WFAN radio to take shots at San Antonio for their halftime shows. “You know at halftime what’s happening tonight? The reunification of the whole Wu-Tang Clan,” Dolan said. “I gotta say, we had Cardi B at halftime for the third game, we have the reunification of Wu-Tang for the fourth game and San Antonio h

item.title: “We were sort of a little negative at 71-42,” the …
item.description: Larry David’s enthusiasm was a little curbed when the Knicks were down 71-42 before making an historic comeback against the Spurs in Game 4 on Wednesday night. Fellow Knicks fan John McEnroe, seated next to David on Celebrity Row at Madison Square Garden, told the ESPN crew on “NBA Today” on Thursday that the “Curb Your Enthusiasm” star was ready to head for the exit when all hope looked lost for the Knicks. “We were sort of a little negative at 71-42,” the former tennis star and ESPN analyst

item.title: The byproduct of a leap of faith by Los Angeles native …
item.description: This is the latest episode of “Attack on Wemby: Across the Knicks-Verse,” the short-form anime series that has provided the most entertaining recap of the NBA Finals. The byproduct of a leap of faith by Los Angeles native Jake Wang, the show, in Japanese with English subtitles, has turned into a popular satire of the storylines surrounding each game of the NBA Finals. It follows Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs as characters from the renowned Japanese anime Attack on Titan who are trying to take

item.title: A wild video showed a mob of youngsters — including at …
item.description: A wild video showed a mob of youngsters — including at least one in blue and orange Knicks merch — launching chairs at a Five Guys employee in Manhattan after Game 4 of the NBA Finals. The violent crew entered the burger joint on West 42nd Street near Seventh Avenue and assaulted a 24-year-old employee around 3 a.m. on Thursday, leaving him bloodied behind the counter, according to the NYPD. Video of the incident posted to social media captured the group of roughly a dozen young men throwing sto

item.title: Some extra attention has been brought to Karl-Anthony …
item.description: One quick hand motion has some in San Antonio up in arms. Some extra attention has been brought to Karl-Anthony Towns’ deflection on the final play of Wednesday’s miracle Knicks Game 4 Finals win after it appears he motioned to the fans sitting courtside to crowd Spurs rookie Dylan Harper for the critical inbounds pass. With the Knicks leading 107-106 with 1.2 seconds remaining, Towns walked toward Harper — positioned on the left sidelines if looking straight at the basket from midcourt — and di

item.title: Draymond Green on Knicks' 29-point comeback: 'The greatest playoff game I've ever seen'
item.description: Draymond Green: By far craziest playoff game I've ever seen. I've been a part of some incredible playoff games myself. That had to be the greatest playoff game I've ever seen. To come back from… because the Spurs were dominating. They were rolling. They were doing everything right. They had to and the Knicks were playing pathetic. And it just started to split slowly but surely. And they kept going, kept going, kept going. When I tell you it got to the point to where when it was in single digits,

item.title: "As a basketball purist, I was happy to see the Knicks …
item.description: Run It Back: Isiah Thomas on Game 4 of the NBA Finals: "As a basketball purist, I was happy to see the Knicks comeback and win. I was terribly upset by the way Spurs played with a 29-point lead."

item.title: Magic Johnson on De'Aaron Fox: “He should’ve pulled …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Magic Johnson on De'Aaron Fox: “He should’ve pulled the ball back out… I think that play really hurt the Spurs. Wemby’s two free throws he missed hurt the spurs. De’Aaron is a point guard, you gotta know every situation. My job as a point guard was to know every situation.” (via @FirstTake , h/t @NBA__Courtside )

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: Everyone on the Spurs know we're going to come back and win this series
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Victor Wembanyama on whether his teammates believe they can come back from down 3-1: “Everybody knows we are going to do it.”

item.title: "I didn't really think much of it. I just saw that one …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Wemby is taking things being tossed at him in stride: "I didn't really think much of it. I just saw that one video of the eggs. I didn't see any other one. It's OK. I don't dislike it. Obviously it's not good at all. But it doesn't bother me."

item.title: "The ability, first and foremost. The fact that we've …
item.description: Maxime Aubin: I've asked Victor Wembanyama about what makes him still confident that the Spurs can bounce back and turn this NBA Finals around: "The ability, first and foremost. The fact that we've proven game after game that nobody beats us — we beat ourselves. The confidence comes from knowing that our destiny is in our own hands."

item.title: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama on NBA Finals Game 4 loss to …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama on NBA Finals Game 4 loss to Knicks: “We’re over it.”

item.title: Mitch Johnson: De'Aaron Fox will have the ball in his hands at the end of the game tomorrow and I have the utmost confidence he will deliver
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Mitch Johnson: "I don't get into social media. I think I've probably been fired 212 times, and we've traded Fox 72 times. People have their opinions. I don't care. De'Aaron Fox will have the ball in his hands at the end of the game tomorrow and I have nothing but the utmost confidence that he's gonna deliver like he's done countless times for us"

item.title: "It's not like people have my phone number and can …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: De’Aaron Fox on the criticism he got after game 4: "It's not like people have my phone number and can call me. I don't watch those shows. It doesn't matter. It is what it is. You can't change it now. We're trying to move on from that"

item.title: "People making money. It's the economy that we live …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: De’Aaron Fox: "People making money. It's the economy that we live in. It's the world that we live in. Am I upset about it? No. Do I understand it? Sure. But I don't think that really changes kind of what happens on the court"

item.title: "I feel like we've made history all year. We've proven …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon Castle on whether they can come back from 3-1: "I feel like we've made history all year. We've proven that with our backs against the wall that we can step up. I don't really expect this to be any different"

item.title: "Definitely a factor but it's the playoffs. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on whether fatigue played a factor at the end of game 4: "Definitely a factor but it's the playoffs. Everybody's just as tired. I mean, it shouldn't even be a factor in the game. I mean, now we got 2 days between games. It's not going to be a factor"

item.title: "At the end of the day, it's just a basketball game. I …
item.description: Sirius XM NBA Radio: "At the end of the day, it's just a basketball game. I don't think anyone should be getting eggs tossed at them." @JulianChampagn2 on the Spurs dealing with Knicks fans outside their hotel

item.title: "Stop on De'Aaron Fox, he's playing with a high ankle …
item.description: Dan Patrick Show: "Stop on De'Aaron Fox, he's playing with a high ankle sprain – probably shouldn't be playing. He would normally dunk that layup – if he would've dunked it, he's the hero..." – John Calipari on people blaming De'Aaron Fox for the #Spurs loss

item.title: Spurs list Kornet as questionable for Game 5 due to an …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs list Kornet as questionable for Game 5 due to an “illness” after he played just 4 minutes in Game 4.

item.title: "We love you and we need you."
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "We love you and we need you." —Pistons head coach JB Bickerstaff on the advice he would give De'Aaron Fox 👏

item.description: NBA Communications: Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals on ABC was the most-watched NBA Finals Game 4 since 1998, averaging 20.9M viewers and peaking with 23.2M viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. The 2026 NBA Finals are the most-watched NBA Finals through four games since 1998.

item.title: Another record-setting audience for the '26 #NBAFinals …
item.description: ESPN PR: Another record-setting audience for the '26 #NBAFinals as ABC drew an audience of 20.9M 👏 🏀 Largest NBA Finals Game 4 audience since '98 🏀 Most-watched NBA Finals Game 4 EVER on ABC 🏀 Up 123% from last year's Game 4 🏀 23.2M peak viewers

item.title: That led multiple observers from other teams to …
item.description: When talking with league insiders in the aftermath of Game 4, there was an emphasis on Spurs rookie guard Dylan Harper, and the potential for him to have greater involvement in the series moving forward, particularly after some late-game issues for De'Aaron Fox. Like throughout the regular season, Harper has come off the bench throughout the Finals, averaging 16.3 points and 6.8 rebounds in the series thus far, including 21 points on 8-for-12 shooting in 32 minutes in Game 4. But Harper was larg

item.title: “You have to take it,” O’Neal told The Post. “If he …
item.description: This postseason Victor Wembanyama has seemingly quieted any talk about who’s the next face of the league, if he hasn’t already snatched that ceremonial title from James, who has held it for two decades. Or has he? “You have to take it,” O’Neal told The Post. “If he wins a championship, yes. If [Jalen] Brunson wins a championship, it’ll be Brunson. Face of the league ain’t something you pass on. You’ve got to go take it.”

item.title: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San …
item.description: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San Antonio ahead of New York’s potentially championship-clinching showdown with the Spurs after stormy weather canceled flights Friday. Orange and blue-clad New Yorkers were ready to invade the second-rate Texas city for Saturday night’s Game 5 of the NBA Finals, but travel plans were thrown into chaos thanks to a raging thunderstorm that swept through the tristate area. Brooklyn resident Toby Hyde was supposed to board a 5:33 p.m. flight to Sa

item.title: “But quickly forget that most assume otherwise. Also.. …
item.description: “But quickly forget that most assume otherwise. Also.. it’s a basketball game.” Some believed Selena Gomez was shading Taylor Swift with her “lol” comment on social media after the Knicks rallied from being down by 29 for a 107-106 win over the Spurs. “Mad respect for the game!! Congrats to the peeps that represent! What a come back,” Gomez wrote over a screenshot of an NBA article of the New York’s historic comeback. “So funny how some are all the sudden fans though lol.”

item.title: The New York Police Department is seeking information …
item.description: The New York Police Department is seeking information on a man suspected of beating a 17-year-old into a coma Wednesday after Game 4 of the NBA Finals. An NYPD statement said a group approached the teen outside Madison Square Garden near 237 W 35th St., and an argument ensued. Police posted a picture of a suspect who allegedly punched and kicked the victim, who suffered a seizure before lapsing into a coma. Police did not release an update on the teen’s condition.

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: European players are a bit dirty
item.description: Draymond Green: Victor Wembanyama is a great player. Victor is a European player. And I've been on record saying European players are dirty and everybody's like Draymond shouldn't say that. And I told y'all no Draymond will [expletive] you up. I'm not dirty. It's a completely different thing. And I don't think anyone that I play against would tell you, man, he dirty. No, I ain't dirty. That's a different thing. I will mess you up though. Victor Wembanyama is a European player. They are a bit dir

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He's been shown a …
item.description: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He's been shown a lot of grace. He probably should be suspended at some point, or have been already. He’s been shown a lot of grace, but I agree with the grace. We want to see Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs versus the New York Knicks, not the San Antonio Spurs sans Victor versus New York Knicks. So, I agree. And I wish the NBA would just come out and say, ‘Yeah, in most cases, this probably should be a suspension, but it's not because we want our

item.title: Officials for Saturday’s Knicks-Spurs Game 5: Scott …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Officials for Saturday’s Knicks-Spurs Game 5: Scott Foster James Capers Tyler Ford Mitchell Ervin (alternate)

item.title: Down 29, to come back versus a team that seemed to be …
item.description: With 1,054 regular-season games and 177 postseason contests, it takes a lot to leave Dwyane Wade feeling “shocked.” And that’s exactly what the Knicks did Wednesday in Game 4 when they set an NBA Finals record by rallying from a 29-point deficit to stun the Spurs and grab a 3-1 series lead. “I was just stunned and shocked like — amazed,” the Basketball Hall of Famer told Page Six in an exclusive interview Friday at the Tribeca Festival in New York City. “I know, I’ve been in the Finals a few tim

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs have slapped a ticket …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs have slapped a ticket restriction on NBA Finals games in a pathetic bid to keep New York fans from flooding the Frost Bank Center. Knicks faithful trying to get into tonight’s Game 5 contest – which could hand New York its first NBA title in 53 years – are being greeted with a warning from Ticketmaster. “Frost Bank Center is located in San Antonio, Texas . Sales to this event will be restricted to customers residing within a 150-mile radius of Frost Bank Center,” the note r

item.title: A Mexican tax official has fouled out of his state …
item.description: A Mexican tax official has fouled out of his state government post. Héctor Contreras Mercader abruptly resigned Thursday as director of the state of Quintana Roo’s Tax Administration Service after a firestorm erupted over how he landed pricey seats near President Trump to watch the Knicks take on the Spurs at Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. Photographers caught Contreras, sporting a Knicks star Jalen Brunson jersey, and his son, wearing a Karl-Anthony Towns jersey, in section

item.title: The Knicks will host watch parties at Radio City Music …
item.description: The Knicks will host watch parties at Radio City Music Hall, Plaza33 outside Madison Square Garden and Wollman Rink in Central Park for Game 5 of the 2026 NBA Finals. The Knicks will face the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 on Saturday, June 13. Tipoff is scheduled for 8:30 p.m.

item.title: A Knicks fan is crying foul after he booked a …
item.description: A Knicks fan is crying foul after he booked a penthouse at the Ritz-Carlton for Game 4 of the NBA Finals — but was allegedly kicked out of the $20,000 hotel room because of a demand from San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama. Pennsylvanian Phil Godlewski was with a party of six, who spent a total of $750,000 on tickets to Wednesday’s game — and rented out one of the two penthouse suites on the 41st floor of the Ritz-Carlton on West 28th Street in NoMad.

item.title: “I hear Ronnie say five words. That’s all he said. …
item.description: The Scranton entrepreneur — who was with his 11 and 9-year-old sons and his friend Ron Pavalonis, and his son — wasn’t told that Wemby was staying in the other penthouse suite. When Pavalonis was entering his room around 5 p.m., he said he saw Wemby exiting his own suite — presumably on his way to Madison Square Garden, and greeted the Spurs star player. “I hear Ronnie say five words. That’s all he said. ‘Good luck tonight, big guy,'” Godlewski, 43, told The Post.

item.title: “I said, ‘What? Why?’ And she said, ‘Because we were …
item.description: 10 minutes later, they got a knock on the door from the hotel’s director of rooms operations, Jean Choi — which they did not hear since their room was so big. So Choi — along with three men in black suits — just let themselves in, Godlewski claimed. “They opened the door illegally, and they actually entered the vestibule area. And she’s yelling from within the room, ‘Mr. Godlewski!’ I finally hear her and walk to the door and that’s when she told me, ‘I’m sorry, but you guys are going to have to

item.title: “Today’s athlete is a lot better and physically …
item.description: “Today’s athlete is a lot better and physically stronger than the athletes of the era that I played in, in any sport. They play a lot more games than we did in the off-season,” he said. He also called the Spurs’ 7′ 4″ powerhouse and now-New York archenemy Victor Wembanyama “a very strong, talented player,” but explained that in his era, a man from France would never play in the NBA. “That’s the other part of the game today, where people are coming into the league from Europe,” he said.

item.title: MSG denies report Spurs are revoking Game 5 tickets to Knicks fans
item.description: Ian Bagley: MSG Sports statement on Game 5 tickets says MSG has confirmed with Spurs ownership that Spurs will not be revoking any Knicks fans’ tickets for tonight.

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He probably should have been suspended at some point already
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green believes Wemby should’ve been suspended already this finals but the NBA is showing him ‘grace’: “I will say he’s been shown a lot of grace. He probably should be suspended at some point or have been already. But I agree with the Grace” (Via @DraymondShow )

item.title: A new name that is very close to signing a contract …
item.description: A new name that is very close to signing a contract with Asvel comes from the NBA. As Meridian Sport has learned, Riley Minix is ​​on the verge of an agreement with the team from Villeurbanne. The American is 25 years old, and was part of San Antonio and Cleveland , as well as their development teams, where he primarily received minutes.

item.title: Carter Bryant on Victor Wembanyama: 'I'm super grateful for Vic'
item.description: "Me and Vic sit next to each other on the plane, so we've had a ton of conversations, whether that was easy conversations, hard conversations, just me checking up on him, him checking up on me," Bryant said. "Sometimes you need a brother to lean on, somebody to hug and somebody to spill out your emotions to and your words to. "We've both been there for each other. I'm super grateful for Vic."

item.title: Bryant did just that, so in Game 7, Wembanyama was …
item.description: Sensing Bryant needed consoling, Wembanyama put his arms on his friend's shoulder's before tapping him on the chest in a "shake it off, stay strong" gesture. Bryant did just that, so in Game 7, Wembanyama was caught on camera telling Bryant "I'm proud of you" while they were on the bench together. "I'm super grateful for Vic," Bryant said. "Vic is an A-1 human being. He's a star on the court, but him as a human being is so much more than he could ever be on the court. And I mean that in the best

item.title: He’s a pleasure to talk to. He’s ever worldly. He’s …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Adam Silver on Wemby: “I’m amazed at Victor. Not just his play on the floor, but he’s such a curious young man. He’s a pleasure to talk to. He’s ever worldly. He’s really dedicated to his craft. He’s got such a bright future ahead of him.”

item.title: I don't know if that's habit, superstition, bathroom, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Mitch Johnson on Wemby not being on the court for the national anthem in the last 2 games: "I think there's plenty of players that are not on the floor during the anthem. I don't know if that's habit, superstition, bathroom, whatever it may be. I don't foresee anything looking into that at all"

item.title: "It's hard, but you just keep trying to tell your guys …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on how to keep the guys locked in after such a crazy Game 4: "It's hard, but you just keep trying to tell your guys to stay present, stay present, stay present, and you know, for us, another thing that we've talked a lot about with our guys is possession over outcome, possession over outcome, just worry about the next possession, lock into the details, play as hard as you can, give everything you can to that next possession, then don't worry about the outcome of the game

item.title: “They told us in our meeting it’s basically going to …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Overheard a security guard at Frost Bank Center: “They told us in our meeting it’s basically going to be a New York Knicks home game.”

item.title: Hopefully we can continue to improve in terms of our …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch Johnson on the vibe before the game: "Went through shoot around and our normal prep for the game tonight, and tonight's the most important game of our season, and we're playing the same opponent now for the fifth straight time, and hopefully we can continue to improve in terms of our execution and finishing out games and playing more to our standard for more of the 48 minutes tonight."

item.title: "We know we can do that. We have to win the next 48"
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on trying to overcome a 3-1 deficit: "We need to take it one game at a time" Haynes: "Not even thinking about the fact that we have to win three?" "We know we can do that. We have to win the next 48"

item.title: "I haven't heard any of them so I don't have to handle …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on handling criticism: "I haven't heard any of them so I don't have to handle them. You're the first one to tell me about it"

item.title: Luke Kornet will be available tonight,
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Luke Kornet will be available tonight, per Mitch Johnson.

item.title: Jalen Brunson on some Knicks' fans being disrespectful: 'I love our fans, but we've got to be better'
item.description: Speaking to NBC News' Jesse Kirsch, Brunson urged Knicks fans to be more disciplined and decent, as New York's celebration of making it back to the Finals stage for the first time in 27 years has been marred by violence and several arrests. "You never want to be disrespectful. Throwing stuff and saying things that are not part of the game is considered disrespectful," the Knicks superstar said. "I love our fans, but we've got to be better."

item.title: San Antonio Spurs are the 1st team in NBA Finals …
item.description: Justin Russo: San Antonio Spurs are the 1st team in NBA Finals history to lead by double-digits at the end of the first quarter 3 different times in the same series.

item.title: Spurs are the first team in Finals history to lead by …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Spurs are the first team in Finals history to lead by 10+ points in the first quarter in the first 5 games. 13 points for NY is the fewest since Golden State had 11 in the 4th quarter in Game 3 of the 2022 Finals.

item.title: New York's 13 first-quarter points are tied for the …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Rough start for the Knicks in Game 5 New York's 13 first-quarter points are tied for the second-fewest in an NBA Finals opening quarter of the shot-clock era.

item.title: Spurs: 3-8 in clutch games in the playoffs
item.description: ‪John Schuhmann‬: Spurs: 3-8 in clutch games in the playoffs.

item.title: Spurs starting guards in Game 5: 4-25 FG 1-11 3P had …
item.description: StatMuse: Spurs starting guards in Game 5: 4-25 FG 1-11 3P had as many buckets as turnovers.

item.title: Knicks win their first NBA championship in 53 years
item.description: Ian Begley: Behind 45 points from Jalen Brunson, Knicks win in San Antonio to clinch franchise's first NBA title in 53 years. Masterpiece from the NYK captain, who had 15 in fourth to help NYK rebound from 7-pt deficit. Massive offensive rebound from Mitchell Robinson late helped seal game

item.title: Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA championship. The better team won. We did a lot of good things and we didn’t finish the job. That's what it is.”

item.title: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of falling short in quest for title: “I don't think anybody other than the people in that room expected us to be here. So there was a lot of good in that & there's a lot of pain in what just happened and both things can be true.”

item.title: Spurs can now start negotiating with Wembanyama on …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: It’s last thing on their minds right now, but the Spurs can now start negotiating with Wembanyama on that big rookie extension.

item.title: Devin Vassell: "First off, to the fans, just thank you …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Devin Vassell: "First off, to the fans, just thank you guys so much for supporting us, especially in my journey, being here six years from where we started, from the 20 wins that we were at to being in the Finals, they've been loyal, and I just want to say thank you to them, because without them, and the noise, and them having our backs, we're not in this situation, we're not in this position. So, just thank you to them."

item.title: Luke Kornet said it was a “joy” to be part of this …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Luke Kornet said it was a “joy” to be part of this team: “We have a lot of great players and a lot of young players and I feel like everyone bought in and were united and about as free and selfless of a group I've ever seen.”

item.title: Bridges walked off the floor in San Antonio to some …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mikal Bridges walked off the floor in San Antonio to some “—- those picks” chants from Knicks fans

item.title: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the NBA Finals — tied for closest finals ever for a series that lasted 5 or fewer games. Celtics were +12 over the Mavericks in the 2024 Finals but that was skewed by a 38-point loss in Game 4. This series was tight the whole way.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: I'm going to have to hold that inside of me and slow down and wait
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama after losing to the Knicks in his first NBA Finals: "What I’m pissed about is that there’s probably 100 games before we can get back to the Finals. I’m going to have to hold that inside of me and slow down and wait."

item.title: Personally, in 18 months, it's been, it's been hard …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Victor Wembanyama: "I don't think we could have learned more and gained more experience in one playoff run and in one season. Personally, in 18 months, it's been, it's been hard and full of lessons."

item.title: "This doesn't compare to anything before, this is the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby: "This doesn't compare to anything before, this is the biggest lesson of my life, the biggest learning moment. I can't tell exactly what the lesson is, but we're learning from that for sure."

item.title: Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites to win the 2027 NBA Finals at +250, followed by the Boston Celtics at +550. The New York Knicks, despite winning the championship on Saturday, have the fourth best odds at +700. The next block of teams have significantly longer odds beginning with the Indiana Pacers and Denver Nuggets at +2800, and the Minnesota Timbewolves, Los Angeles Lakers, Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers at +3000.

item.title: If they stopped the games after 46 minutes, the Spurs …
item.description: Dean Oliver: If my count is right, if they stopped the games after 46 minutes, the Spurs would have won the series in 5 games.

item.title: the Spurs led for 69% of the NBA Finals
item.description: Esfandiar Baraheni: Insane stat by the ESPN broadcast: the Spurs led for 69% of the NBA Finals. Closest 5-game series in the Finals I can ever think of.

item.title: Lots of love and support for each other coming out of …
item.description: Kelly Iko: Lots of love and support for each other coming out of that Spurs locker room. Those guys will be around for a while.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on losing NBA Finals: 'It's extremely painful, but I'm not running away from that'
item.description: NBA Base: Victor Wembanyama when asked about greats like Michael Jordan and LeBron James taking years to win their first ring: "It’s extremely painful. It’s painful, but I’m not running away from that. I’m using that to fuel me. I'm sure all these guys you named, they're not satisfied with being eliminated in earlier rounds or not making the playoffs, and I'm not satisfied with not winning."

item.title: Dylan Harper falls 27 points short of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's rookie playoff scoring record
item.description: Jorge Sierra: With Spurs falling in five, Dylan Harper will not be able to beat Kareem's record for most playoff points in a rookie season. He fell 27 points short of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's 352. By the way, Kareem set the record in just 10 playoff games.

item.title: Clarkson became the first NBA player with Filipino …
item.description: Jordan Clarkson has made Philippine basketball history. Clarkson became the first NBA player with Filipino descent to win a championship in the NBA, after the New York Knicks clinched the coveted title on Sunday (Manila time). The Knicks hacked out a 94-90 win over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the Finals at the Frost Bank Center in Texas.

item.title: Knicks players smoking cigars on the Spurs court after …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Knicks players smoking cigars on the Spurs court after winning an NBA championship. Unreal scenes.

item.title: Mitch Johnson: 'We weren't ready to win an NBA championship'
item.description: Mitch Johnson: 'We weren't ready to win an NBA championship' Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA championship. The better team won. We did a lot of good things and we didn’t finish the job. That's what it is.”

item.title: Devin Vassell says Dylan Harper was frustrated with his role with Spurs
item.description: Legion Hoops: Devin Vassell says Dylan Harper was frustrated with his role and playing time with the Spurs the season. “But when we needed him most he stepped up.” (h/t @big_business_)

item.title: Harper, after embracing each of his teammates in the …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Dylan Harper, after embracing each of his teammates in the locker room: "We all learned a lot in this playoff run. A lot of people counted us out and we proved people wrong. It didn't go how we wanted to go, but we definitely learned a lot."

item.title: Stephon Castle on how the Knicks defense made it tough …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Stephon Castle on how the Knicks defense made it tough on him and his teammates: “They crowded the pick and rolls a lot, made us stagnant on the ball. When we tried get to isolation, they took away passing lanes too, made us play one-on-one a lot.”

item.title: Spurs somehow won the clutch time minutes in this …
item.description: Jace frederick: Fun fact: Spurs somehow won the clutch time minutes in this series 39-38 Shot 38% in the clutch to New York’s 32%

item.title: One Knicks executive arrived prepared for the moment, …
item.description: The bottles had already popped. The floor of the San Antonio Spurs’ visiting locker room was soaked in champagne. One Knicks executive arrived prepared for the moment, carrying with him a brown, wooden box of 53 celebratory cigars, one for each year that had passed since the organization’s last title. The Knicks had closed out a 94-90 victory Saturday night in Game 5. They were champions. And Anunoby, who just reeled off a playoff run that will go down in history, if only because it included one

item.title: “Those are my brothers for life,” Hart said. “We have …
item.description: But eight years later, the college teammates became legends together again, leading the Knicks to their first championship in 53 years with a 94-90 win over the Spurs in Game 5 of the NBA Finals at Frost Bank Center. They are the first trio of teammates to win an NCAA title (2016) and an NBA title together. “Those are my brothers for life,” Hart said. “We have a bond that’ll never be broken. We won a championship together in college, but this one obviously takes the cake. “We’ve been built for t

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's latest 2026 NBA Finals defeat …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama's latest 2026 NBA Finals defeat continued a painful career pattern of second-place finishes across youth tournaments, France, the Olympics, the NBA Cup, and now the NBA Finals.

item.title: LeBron James remains the youngest NBA player ever to …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Jalen Brunson's 45-point game means Victor Wembanyama ended up not leading the playoffs in scoring. So LeBron James remains the youngest NBA player ever to top the postseason in points.

item.title: The Spurs outscored the Knicks by 5.4 points per …
item.description: ?NBA Stat?: The Spurs outscored the Knicks by 5.4 points per 100 possessions with Dylan Harper on the court the during the NBA Finals: On: +5.4 Net Rating Off: -8 Net Rating Mitch Johnson cost the Spurs any shot at making this a competitive series not playing Harper over De’Aaron Fox. (via E. Sidery) #NBA

item.title: “He wasn’t ready. I know if they haven’t already, this …
item.description: Iman Shumpert says it’s time for Wemby to work with Tim Duncan to find a go to move: “He wasn’t ready. I know if they haven’t already, this is your year with Tim Duncan. It’s time to sit down with Tim Duncan for the whole summer and understand how to get to something. How to have a plan that nobody can do anything about… He’s gonna have to watch Dirk, you’re gonna have to watch some Kobe. Go watch some Tim Duncan”

item.title: 7"6 and no post game or back to the basket game. Wild …
item.description: David West: 7"6 and no post game or back to the basket game. Wild business.

item.title: Coach Brown and the Knicks staff out coached the Spurs …
item.description: David West: I know the players played but Coach Brown and the Knicks staff out coached the Spurs staff. It was a mismatch.

item.title: The Knicks and Spurs have been must-listen as much as …
item.description: The Knicks and Spurs have been must-listen as much as must-watch this NBA Finals, according to new data from Spotify. NBA Finals-related podcast listening on Spotify surged more than 350% over the past week, while streams of Finals-related episodes were up more than 450% over the same period. The morning after Game 3 — Tuesday, June 9 — was the single biggest day for sports podcast listening on the platform this year, up roughly 500% from typical levels.

item.title: Mamdani’s team reached out to MSG ahead of the game …
item.description: The young Democratic socialist mayor claimed he paid “roughly $1,000” for his nosebleeds standing-room spot for Monday night’s Game 3 between the Knicks and San Antonio Spurs, though sources with knowledge of the matter said the ticket actually cost him $750. Regular plebs had to shell out an average of around $7,500 for a ticket to the high-profile event. But Mamdani’s team reached out to MSG ahead of the game and told reps the mayor wanted a half-dozen tickets to attend the NBA Finals ahead of

item.title: A teen was beaten into a coma by a brute bellowing …
item.description: A teen was beaten into a coma by a brute bellowing “Spurs in 7” during a livestreamed Midtown brawl as Game 4 of the NBA finals neared its triumphant end for the Knicks, cops and sources said. Johnny Aumuller, 17, was knocked around by a crew that included a rabble-rouser in a black-and-white striped shirt around 11:45 p.m. Wednesday at West 35th Street near Eighth Avenue — a short walk from Madison Square Garden, law enforcement sources said. “I have no words for him,” Johnny’s mother, Shiomara

item.title: While the teen victim was not the one who made that …
item.description: The teen victim was part of a group that gathered to watch the game when the aggressor declared, “Spurs are up right now. We’re winning. Spurs in 7,” according to the sources. That set off a Knicks fan in the crowd, who snapped back, “F–k the Spurs. Suck my d–k,” the sources said. While the teen victim was not the one who made that comment, he was with the Knicks fan’s crew — and a violent brawl erupted in which he soon became the target, the sources said.

item.title: Johnny was pummeled by a ruthless mob, before the …
item.description: Johnny was pummeled by a ruthless mob, before the initial Spurs fan picked him up and threw him on the ground, according to the sources. The badly battered teen, who sources say divides his time between the Bronx and Connecticut, suffered a seizure and went into a coma, cops said. He was rushed to Bellevue Hospital, where he eventually came out of the coma and was listed in critical but stable condition, cops and sources said.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns on Victor Wembanyama: Once-in-a-generation athlete
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Karl-Anthony Towns on Wemby: "He's going to be something special for a lot of years to come. Once-in-a-generation athlete. "Shout out to the Spurs, too. They're the real deal. "Shout out to Jersey's own, Dylan Harper; he's the real deal."

item.title: Brunson on the Spurs: “That's a hell of a group. …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Jalen Brunson on the Spurs: “That's a hell of a group. That's a well-coached (team). The culture they have is clearly evident. They have it. And so a lot of respect to them and what they brought to the table. I'm just happy were able to find ways to win four games."

item.title: "Yea I think so, don't want to sit here and be like …
item.description: Cory Mose: Spurs locker room spoke about losing double digit leads in every game against the Knicks... I asked De'Aaron Fox if that was the most frustrating part about this series "Yea I think so, don't want to sit here and be like you were the better team but you know every game we were able to build up this lead and kind of just watch it wither away"

item.title: “This league needs to go back to leaning on veterans. …
item.description: SportsCenter: “This league needs to go back to leaning on veterans. … Towards the end you watched the Spurs struggle.” Iman Shumpert on the role the Knicks’ veteran leadership played in the Finals.

item.title: Shaq says Victor Wembanyama needs to hit the weight …
item.description: NBA Base: Shaq says Victor Wembanyama needs to hit the weight room this summer after losing to the Knicks: “As good as he is, he’s going to need to get stronger. Because when you become a dominant big man, people are going to force…. So, I don’t know what he’s going to do this summer, but he definitely needs to get in that weight room and get stronger.”

item.title: And it joins only Toronto in 2018-19, according to the …
item.description: This team Leon Rose built is the NBA's first to win the Larry O’Brien Trophy and NBA Cup double in the same season. It just beat the Spurs four times in five tries despite spotting a double-digit lead to San Antonio in the first quarter of all five games. And it joins only Toronto in 2018-19, according to the Elias Sports Bureau, as NBA champions with four starters acquired via trade. These Knicks and those Raptors are also the league's only two teams, per Elias, to open a Finals-clinching game

item.title: Spurs had 99.6% win odds in Game 4 up 20 in the 4th. …
item.description: Justin Van Zuiden: Spurs had 92.3% win odds in Game 1 up 13 in the 3rd. Spurs had 83.6% win odds in Game 2 up 12 in the 2nd. Spurs had 99.6% win odds in Game 4 up 20 in the 4th. Spurs had 97.1% win odds in Game 5 up 14 in the 3rd. They lost them all.

item.title: Wembanyama's playoff averages of 23.8 points, 10.9 …
item.description: The Spurs succumbed to the same maddening script in their Game 5 loss, squandering a 16-point lead to dash Victor Wembanyama's dream of winning his first championship. Wembanyama was active early and finished with 19 points, 14 rebounds and five blocks, but he faded again with the season on the line. Brunson, the Finals MVP, outscored him 15-3 in the series-deciding fourth quarter. Nevertheless, Wembanyama ranked first in Player Efficiency Rating (26.3) during his first postseason run and led al

item.title: Kendrick Perkins: Victor Wembanyama, Stephon Castle and Dylan Harper are untouchable
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "Wemby, Stephon Castle and Dylan Harper [are untouchable]. The rest of the roster needs to be reevaluated." — @KendrickPerkins on how the Spurs can contend for a title next season 🏆 👀

item.title: Despite their dominance, oddsmakers still have the …
item.description: The Knicks got off to a rocky start in the first round against the Atlanta Hawks but found their rhythm and won 15 of 16 games to clinch their first championship since 1973. They capped the run by knocking off the favored Spurs in five games in the Finals and finished with by far the largest point differential (+283) in a postseason run in NBA history, according to ESPN Research. Despite their dominance, oddsmakers still have the Knicks behind the Spurs (+250), Thunder (+260) and Celtics (+550)

item.title: Pau Gasol: Congratulations to the @nyknicks on a …
item.description: Pau Gasol: Congratulations to the @nyknicks on a historical run to win the @NBA championship! 🏆 Much love to the @spurs for an amazing run! They will come back stronger next season without a doubt 💪🏼

item.title: Tempers flared with one fan sporting a Dennis Rodman …
item.description: It was a tough scene for Spurs fans after their Game 5 loss to the Knicks Saturday night. Tempers flared with one fan sporting a Dennis Rodman Spurs jersey. Video went viral of him trying to fight every Knicks fan in his immediate vicinity inside Moynihan Train Hall at Penn Station, shoving and chasing down multiple people while throwing a few punches before a police officer came over to try to calm him down. Cops then escorted the fan away while Knicks fans taunted him.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on what he learned in the Finals: 'The margin of error is very, very thin'
item.description: “One of many things I learned is the margin of error is very, very thin,” Wembanyama said after his season ended with just 19 points on 7-for-19 shooting. “Our domination stints are absolute. We absolutely dominated for most of the series. But our errors, our mistakes, are punished so hard that we can’t have ups and downs like this so much.”

item.title: There's no guarantee that this group will ever have …
item.description: "I think you have to look at it for the season it's been," veteran forward Harrison Barnes told Yahoo Sports. "The pain of losing in the Finals, and ultimately you have to accept that. There's no guarantee that this group will ever have the opportunity to achieve that. Some players in this room may be able to get back to the Finals, some players in this room may be able to win a championship. I think accepting what this moment has been, where we come to as a team, and hopefully as guys continue

item.title: Lawrence Jones fulfills a bet made on the NBA Finals, …
item.description: Lawrence Jones fulfills a bet made on the NBA Finals, eating sardines live on air after his San Antonio Spurs team lost to Brian Kilmeade's New York Knicks.

item.title: A teenager was shot, four people were stabbed and five …
item.description: Massive crowds brought chaos to the streets of New York City after the New York Knicks clinched the team's first NBA championship in 53 years. After the final buzzer confirmed the Knicks' 94-90 victory over the San Antonio Spurs at Texas' Frost Bank Center on Saturday, June 13, throngs of people sporting blue and orange flocked to the streets of all five boroughs. Amid the raucous celebration — which also followed the first World Cup game in the N.Y.C. area — a teenager was shot, four people wer

item.title: No other pair of teammates in @NBA history has …
item.description: Crazy Stats: Victor Wembanyama and Stephon Caste finished the 2026 postseason with combined totals of 942 points, 355 rebounds, 200 assists, 42 steals, and 86 blocks. No other pair of teammates in @NBA history has combined to produce as many points, rebounds, assists, steals, and blocks in a single playoff run, and only 11 duos have exceeded four of those marks.

item.title: One clip showed Sochan, while shirtless, nearly …
item.description: The Knicks clinching their first championship since 1973 on Saturday brought joy to many, but one of their most recent acquisitions might’ve been the happiest of them all. Following the Knicks’ Saturday Game 5 victory over the Spurs, 94-90, which sealed their first NBA championship win in 53 years, several clips of Jeremy Sochan celebrating went viral. One clip showed Sochan, while shirtless, nearly toppling over after trying to climb the giant Larry O’Brien trophy on the floor at the Frost Bank

item.title: The Bulls landed on Splitter due to his ability to …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Bulls landed on Splitter due to his ability to lead the team’s player development, organizational alignment on the franchise's direction and vision, and his leadership and knowledge base as a coach rising through the ranks since 2018 as well as a seven-year NBA player with one championship with the San Antonio Spurs. Splitter, 41, guided the Trail Blazers to a 42-40 record and a postseason berth as the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference amid adversity stemming from Chauncey

item.title: Immediately following the Knicks’ win over the Spurs …
item.description: Sales of Knicks championship gear has already more than doubled that of Fanatics’ previous best-selling NBA Finals champion: the LeBron James-led Los Angeles Lakers in 2020. Immediately following the Knicks’ win over the Spurs on Saturday night, Fanatics released more than 300 championship-branded products. The company took in more than 8,000 orders per minute after the Knicks’ 4-1 series win, also a record.

item.title: Spurs remain committed to De'Aaron Fox
item.description: "I know there's a lot of interesting in whether they might do something with De'Aaron Fox, but they are committed to him right now as their franchise point guard," said Brian Windhorst on Monday's Sports Center. "And I think they will remain that way."

item.title: "He’s a once in a generation player that the league is …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Karl-Anthony Towns when asked about Victor Wembanyama not being “friendly” during the Finals "He’s a once in a generation player that the league is honored to have." (h/t @MrBuckBuckNBA)

item.title: “I wanted to [retaliate] but at the same time, I knew …
item.description: Host Whoopi Goldberg asked Brunson how he resisted retaliating against Victor Wembanyama after the Spurs star shoved Brunson to the ground in Game 3. “I think it starts, honestly, with my parents, how they raised me,” the Knicks star said. “I’ll start there. “I wanted to [retaliate] but at the same time, I knew that being a leader, understanding the moment, understanding the situation, you have to keep your composure. No matter when it’s being too high or being too low, you gotta stay even-keele

item.title: Draymond Green on Spurs not congratulating Knicks after elimination: I hate it, they lacked a leader to say that's not what you do
item.description: “To see [the Spurs] walk off the court, it was disheartening. I’ll blame it on youth,” Green said. “I’ll blame it on lacking the leader to show them that ‘hey, this is what you do. Not walk off.’ I blame it on that. “There’s a way to win and there’s a way to lose. And walking off the court, not looking your killer in his eyes, ain’t the way to lose…I hate it. I hate when people do it. And I hated to see those young Spurs do it because I actually think they’re capable of doing something special…a

item.title: I would have loved for the Spurs players to be there …
item.description: “Oh, by the way, y’all ain’t going to come shake [Jalen Brunson’s] hand?” Green said. “No problem. What’s the first thing Jalen Brunson did? Beeline to [Spurs coach] Mitch Johnson. Respect. Much respect. I would have loved for the Spurs players to be there for them to shake [the Knicks’] hands. They walked off. That was disappointing, a bit disappointing.”

item.title: I can confirm it was Wemby’s idea.
item.description: Maxime Aubin: I can confirm it was Wemby’s idea. Last Saturday, after the loss, he asked if he could visit a children’s hospital before heading back to France.

item.description: While the Knicks' starters made the media rounds after taking down the San Antonio Spurs 4-1 in the NBA Finals, one stop they made came on Good Morning America, along with a trip to the Today Show. While both clips were worth watching as the five Knicks attempted to do the interviews on no sleep, one moment on the Today Show stood out as Brunson revealed what his longtime teammate, Hart, texted to him and Bridges. (Begins at around the 3:50 mark of the video below) "[Josh] had a great text the o

item.description: According to ESPN's Bobby Marks, the Spurs have the financial flexibility to be active in free agency and could target a stretch forward capable of complementing Wembanyama. "The Spurs have the flexibility to go out and do some free-agency shopping," Marks said during ESPN's NBA Today. "When you look at the playoffs, they need a combo big man who can complement Victor [Wembanyama]. When you look at a stretch 4, a player like Rui Hachimura from the Lakers, you pair him up with a big who can shoot

item.title: Victor Wembanyama finished the 2026 postseason with …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Victor Wembanyama finished the 2026 postseason with 524 points, 239 rebounds, 59 assists, 21 steals, and 78 blocks. Only one player in league history has exceeded all five marks in a single playoff run: Hakeem Olajuwon (664/254/98/40/92 in 1994). (via CrazyStats) #NA

item.description: Crazy Stats: Dylan Harper became the youngest player in @NBA history to score 5, 10, 15, 20, and 25 points in a Finals game.
