item.description: NBACentral: Rich Paul says De’Aaron Fox is Tony Parker, Stephon Castle is Kawhi Leonard, Dylan Harper is Manu Ginóbili, and Victor Wembanyama is Tim Duncan

item.title: Texas Rangers Sports Network looking to acquire NBA local broadcast rights
item.description: The Texas Rangers Sports Network is trying to acquire the local broadcast rights to the various NBA teams that exited Main Street Sports Group, through a mostly direct-to-distributor model that somewhat mirrors Fubo’s failed attempt to do the same.

item.title: Spurs and others are still weighing the Rangers’ …
item.description: Surprised to be solicited by an MLB franchise, some of the NBA teams have passed -- such as the Thunder, who, according to industry insiders, control their own local ad inventory and aren’t interested in a revenue share. But sources said potentially the Spurs and others are still weighing the Rangers’ blueprint that projects to pay teams approximately $15M or more annually and also includes a flexible direct-to-consumer streaming product.

item.title: According to one NBA franchise that wanted an OTA …
item.description: The network is also promising some NBA teams it would televise a portion of their games -- perhaps as many as 15 -- on local over-the-air linear channels. According to one NBA franchise that wanted an OTA option adjacent to direct-to-distributor, the Rangers’ sales pitch emphasized increased audience reach through free broadcasts and included OTA. But sources said not every team sees OTA broadcasts as a must and that the Rangers network also doesn’t necessarily consider it mandatory. In other wo

item.title: Harrison Barnes: Year 14 in the books 📚 GRATEFUL is …
item.description: Harrison Barnes: Year 14 in the books 📚 GRATEFUL is the word I’d chose for this season. Rest and reflect for next season.

item.title: NBA says Knicks, Spurs were most watched NBA Finals in 28 years
item.description: NBA Communications: Knicks-Spurs was the most-watched NBA Finals in 28 years, averaging 20.6 million viewers per game on ABC and ESPN. The Knicks’ series-clinching win in Game 5 averaged 24.5 million viewers and peaked with 33 million viewers — marking the most-watched Game 5 in 28 years. The league also had its most-watched postseason in 28 years across ABC/ESPN, Amazon Prime Video and NBC/Peacock.

item.title: Jones has asked Austin billionaire Michael Dell to …
item.description: Mayor Gina Ortiz Jones wants the world’s sixth-richest person to help pay for the Spurs’ planned $1.3 billion downtown arena. Jones has asked Austin billionaire Michael Dell to consider paying some — if not all — of the city’s pledged $489 million contribution to the project.

item.title: “Normal folks in the community have routinely asked, …
item.description: “I think it’s only fair,” Jones said of her request to Dell. “Normal folks in the community have routinely asked, be it at public comment or at other forums, ‘Why can’t we ask them (billionaires) to pay more.’ So, I in fact did that.”

item.title: Patrick Beverley: "I think D'Aaron Fox was trash. I …
item.description: Patrick Beverley: "I think D'Aaron Fox was trash. I think he was god-awful. And I don't think that was because of him. I think because he was injured a little bit. I think the coach should have had him come off the bench and kind of went with what he had against OKC. I didn't like him just putting him in the starting lineup. But you live and you learn, and that's the difference between good coaches and elite coaches. There's no disrespect to coach Mitch, but elite coaches would have put D'Aaron

item.title: His substitution pattern was horrible throughout the …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "I voted for Mitch Johnson for coach of the year. I thought he did an outstanding job in the regular season, even throughout the course of the playoffs. The number one thing was his substitution pattern was horrible throughout the Finals. I go back to Game 3, why was Dylan Harper on the bench in and out of the lineup so much in that fourth quarter when they had that collapse? I mean in Game 4, he was 21 points, eight for 12 from the field, plus 12 on the stat sheet, and I just

item.title: If I’m mad because I lost, do something about it. But …
item.description: Draymond Green on Spurs players not shaking hands with Knicks: If I’m mad because I lost, do something about it. But the time to do it something about it actually isn’t to walk off the court because you had a chance to do something about it and you lost. So to walk off the court to me it ain’t about sportsmanship. That’s about we just looked each other in the eyes five, six, seven games in a row. And now that I beat you, you’re afraid to look me in my eyes. Remember what I said? If you leave, I

item.title: It was just kind of instinct, like how I was raised. I …
item.description: NBA Base: Jalen Brunson on why he went to hug Spurs HC Mitch Johnson before his father after the win: "I hugged and said what's up to Coach Johnson from the Spurs first, just to show respect… It was just kind of instinct, like how I was raised. I think win or loss, you show respect regardless of the outcome. I’ve got a lot of respect for them over there." (Via @CBSMornings)

item.title: Sweeney often worked with the bigs, especially Dereck …
item.description: Grant Afseth: Riley Crean spent a lot of time learning from Sean Sweeney when they were both with the Dallas Mavericks. Sweeney often worked with the bigs, especially Dereck Lively II. After Sweeney joined the San Antonio Spurs, Crean often worked with the bigs.

item.title: Madison Square Garden has been hit with a proposed …
item.description: Madison Square Garden has been hit with a proposed class action lawsuit alleging hackers accessed sensitive visitor data from up to 26 million people collected through controversial surveillance and facial-recognition systems used at the Knicks home arena. The suit against Madison Square Garden Entertainment was filed Tuesday in New York federal court, one day after cybercrime group ShinyHunters claimed to have hacked the company’s internal systems to obtain everything from biometric facial reco

item.title: "This is the weirdest Finals I’ve ever seen in my life …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon: "This is the weirdest Finals I’ve ever seen in my life ... I was 0-5 on what was going to happen and how it was going to happen, even during the games. The resilience the Knicks had getting down 15 points in every game whether it was home or on the road was wild."

item.title: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics
item.description: Shamit Dua: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics. Meyer comes from the Suns with previous stints in Dallas and San Antonio.

item.title: “The Magic were the most thorough and they did the …
item.description: Sean Sweeney landed interviews for more than a few NBA head coaching vacancies over the last few years, and many of them seemed the same. Talking to Orlando, he said, was different. Sweeney was introduced Thursday as the new coach of the Magic, which was the obvious proof that he won them over by his answers during the interview process. Turns out, the Magic won him over with their questions as well. “The Magic were the most thorough and they did the best job of asking questions and asking follo

item.title: “It was during the playoffs and not only was he able …
item.description: He stayed locked in on his tasks with the Spurs, even though the interview process started early in their postseason run. But with the finals over, Sweeney wasted little time in getting to Orlando and starting on the new job. “When we finally did get in front of him, it was a long day and he had a lot going on,” Magic President Jeff Weltman said. “It was during the playoffs and not only was he able to kind of compartmentalize his work with the Spurs and present well to us, but he really knocked

item.title: “Coach Pop’s office was right next to mine and I was …
item.description: “Coach Pop’s office was right next to mine and I was very fortunate,” Sweeney said. “When you’re next to a guy who’s the greatest coach of all time daily, the fact that he even said hello and knew my name meant something to me. So yeah, Coach Pop, he built that program and guys like me are fortunate to be part of it. I’m very grateful and that’s part of the reason why I’m here now.”

item.title: Oscar Wembanyama on living in his brother Victor's shadow: 'It's definitely not easy'
item.description: Coming from a basketball family, and with Victor Wembanyama as your brother, do you feel pressure from the comparisons, or do you tune out the noise? Oscar Wembanyama: I mean, it's definitely not easy. I think every athlete that has a family member that went pro knows what it is. People recognize you without you doing nothing. But I think I just don't care about a lot of things. social media, people knowing you. Like, I don't care about that. I care about the people that are close to me. So I th

item.title: Spurs remain committed to De'Aaron Fox as their franchise starting point guard
item.description: Internally, the Spurs remain committed to Fox as their franchise starting point guard. Sources called De’Aaron Fox a calming presence and the team's closer for most of the season, adding that one rough series essentially playing on one leg doesn't change that. The four-year, $229 million contract extension Fox signed last summer kicks in next season, raising his salary from $37.1 million to $49.8 million. He's scheduled to make $61.7 million in the final year of the deal in 2029-30, when he'll b

item.title: San Antonio could use a backup stretch big as it …
item.description: San Antonio could use a backup stretch big as it reloads for next season, and a combo stretch big man who can start and possibly play alongside Wembanyama in bigger lineups such as Rui Hachimura. The opportunity to play alongside Wembanyama, the franchise's success, its culture and collective youth make San Antonio a preferred destination, sources said, adding that multiple big men are already trying to get to south Texas.

item.title: Paul Pierce says whatever the Lakers do this …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce says whatever the Lakers do this offseason, they have to get more athletic: “Because you’re looking at OKC and the Spurs, their perimeters, their centers. Like, you have to get more athletic and you have to get faster. Like, you can’t be an old team. You need somebody that can get up and down the court and pick up. Because it’s major guard play in the West. You got to focus on your guard play. Do you see what’s running the league right now? Brunson just won MVP. You go

item.title: Oscar Wembanyama on being compared to brother Victor: 'It's definitely not easy'
item.description: Coming from a basketball family, and with Victor Wembanyama as your brother, do you feel pressure from the comparisons, or do you tune out the noise? Oscar Wembanyama: I mean, it's definitely not easy. I think every athlete that has a family member that went pro knows what it is. People recognize you without you doing nothing. But I think I just don't care about a lot of things. social media, people knowing you. Like, I don't care about that. I care about the people that are close to me. So I th

item.title: Carmelo Anthony on De’Aaron Fox in the Finals: As a young veteran he should understand the pace of the game
item.description: Carmelo Anthony: I think De'Aaron Fox as a young veteran, somebody who's been around this game for a while should understand that the pace of the game, understand the momentum of the game, which is why he's there, right? He's there to control the game, make big shots. He's a closer, too. This is what he does. These shots are shots that De'Aaron Fox usually take and make. So, when you have the trust factor is so high in San Antonio that Mitch trusts is De'Aaron Fox to go out there and win him bas

item.title: San Antonio’s recent thrilling Spurs playoff run …
item.description: San Antonio’s recent thrilling Spurs playoff run delivered an estimated $36.2 million in economic impact from game ticket sales and visitor spending on hotels, food and transportation, according to Visit San Antonio. The tourism agency said the estimate also includes a boost to San Antonio businesses and media exposure from national and international television outlets.

item.title: San Antonio hotels and tourism saw a significant surge …
item.description: Visit San Antonio broke down the projected impact by playoff round, putting the first two rounds at $3.5 million each, the Western Conference finals at nearly $6.8 million, and the NBA Finals — with the study projected for a seven-game series — at about $22 million. San Antonio hotels and tourism saw a significant surge of business from New York Knicks fans who traveled to watch Game 1, Game 2 and Game 5 at the Frost Bank Center.

item.title: San Antonio easily led all local markets for the NBA …
item.description: San Antonio easily led all local markets for the NBA Finals on ABC/ESPN with a 25.9 local rating across five games. N.Y. was No. 2 with a 17.5 local rating for the games. That’s well above what the N.Y. market delivered for the five-game World Series with the Yankees and Dodgers in 2024 on Fox (12.2 local rating). Rounding out the top markets for the NBA Finals were Austin, Oklahoma City and Richmond-Petersburg, Va.

item.title: Kevin Garnett: Victor Wembanyama should focus on core strength
item.description: Kevin Garnett on Victor Wembanyama: One thing I want to speak on is just Wemby's development. If I'm him, I’m working on core strength because when… Paul will tell you, I'm not the biggest guy, right? But he'll tell you when I sit down and post up, can you move me? Paul Pierce: No, he's strong. Garnett: I'm probably one of the strongest people you could ever run up on and look like how I look, but when it came down there to setting up on that block and then pitching and getting it off down there

item.title: Jared McCain on Victor Wembanyama: I was going to ask …
item.description: Jared McCain on Victor Wembanyama: I was going to ask for Wemby's jersey, but like now that he sent a hit on me, I can’t. Like, I don't know if I… Friend: That's got to be the thumbnail for the video. Wemby sent a hit on me and… McCain: Wemby tried to kill me… Gone wrong.

item.title: Doncic admitted he did not wake up in the middle of …
item.description: The New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs 4-1 in the NBA Finals, claiming their first championship since 1973. Brunson was named Finals MVP after averaging 32.6 points, 4.2 rebounds, and 4.6 assists in the series. However, because of the time difference in Slovenia, Luka Doncic admitted he did not wake up in the middle of the night to watch the games live. "It's all too early for me, waking up at three in the morning, so I didn't watch," Doncic said with a smile, via Siol.net.

item.title: "They beat OKC...that was a mountaintop for them. I …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Josh Hart on watching the Spurs celebrate their series win vs the Thunder to go to the finals: "They beat OKC...that was a mountaintop for them. I look at JB I was like you see that reaction because they think they gonna win it. They think it's over"

item.title: After being consistent role players for Oklahoma City …
item.description: After being consistent role players for Oklahoma City throughout the past season, league sources say opposing executives have circled the wagon on Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins. Both players saw limited action against the Spurs in the Conference Finals, despite being key contributors during the regular season, and would represent significant depth upgrades for most teams around the league.

item.title: I'm a competitor too, but I also like to stare my …
item.description: In a recent appearance on the Breakfast Club Power 105.1 FM, Jose Alvarado also expressed disappointment with how Victor Wembanyama did not even shake his and the New York Knicks' hands after the 2026 NBA Finals. "Wemby is a great player. ... I mean, I got mixed emotions. I'm a competitor too, but I also like to stare my enemies down. I look forward to them, and I shake their hand. Obviously, it's a game. You don't like the moment you lost your pride, the biggest game of your career, but you're

item.title: The celebrated basketball from British forward OG …
item.description: The celebrated basketball from British forward OG Anunoby’s legendary game-winning tip-in during Game 4 of the NBA Finals 2026 is officially heading to Sotheby’s, with experts projecting the artifact could fetch up to $3 million. This iconic piece of sports memorabilia represents the crowning moment of the New York Knicks’ first championship run in 53 years, sealing one of many dramatic comeback victories against the San Antonio Spurs during their best-of-seven championship series in the 2026 NB

item.title: “I believe if they had Chris Paul on this roster, they …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Isaiah Thomas says if the Spurs kept Chris Paul they would’ve won the NBA Finals: “I believe if they had Chris Paul on this roster, they would’ve won the finals… His experience & who he is would’ve helped De’Aaron Fox, Castle, Harper.”

item.title: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. …
item.description: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. That’s 150 first-round picks. First question: did they make an impact for more than a month or two for the team that drafted them? The obvious choices are Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball (2020); Cade Cunningham (2021) and Stephon Castle (2024). Throw in guys like Onyeka Okongwu (2020) and Anthony Black (2023) who clearly are difference makers for the teams that drafted them or traded for them on draft night. How many of that 150 total made t

item.title: Warriors have discussed No. 11 pick to Pelicans for Trey Murphy
item.description: Golden State has also been one of many interested suitors in New Orleans Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, and the two teams have discussed concepts involving the No. 11 pick, league sources told HoopsHype. As previously reported by HoopsHype, the Pelicans have searched for a trade into the lottery and are targeting a player. Nate Ament has been mentioned as one potential candidate for the Pelicans. Before this past trade deadline, several teams, including the Warriors, Pistons, Pacers, Hawks, T

item.title: Cedric Coward: "Memphis has gotten a bad rap"
item.description: HH: With this young core and picks No. 3 and 16, where do you see this team’s trajectory? Cedric Coward: 'I see a team that’s trying to develop talent where we can compete with the Spurs, the Thunder, and teams of that caliber that are championship-level teams. For me, I can focus on what I do on the court on a day-to-day basis and how I treat my own work in terms of making sure I’m on top of it and then that will trickle down to helping me be a leader and making sure they’re putting in their ow

item.title: Kawhi Leonard says his stoicism comes from playing with the Spurs
item.description: The stoicism, it turns out, has an origin story. “I think it started off just being on the Spurs,” he says, “just seeing the older vets and how they carried themselves and what they wanted their players to act like on the floor.” The Spurs, under Gregg Popovich, famously ran one of the most understated franchises in professional sports — a culture shaped in no small part by Tim Duncan, who won five championships and retired without ever seeming particularly interested in being celebrated for it.

item.title: The 7-foot-3 Mara has been billed as a potential …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The 7-foot-3 Mara has been billed as a potential Victor Wembanyama stopper. Pretty perfect for him to go to this Thunder team that could certainly use one.

item.title: Spurs select Jayden Quaintance at No. 20
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The San Antonio Spurs will select Jayden Quaintance with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: There were a lot of medical red flags coming up for …
item.description: Jake Fischer: There were a lot of medical red flags coming up for Jayden Quaintance, with many teams scared about his troublesome knee. San Antonio has the opportunity to slow roll his return from a torn ACL.

item.title: Tarris Reed Jr goes to Spurs at No. 35 after trade with Nuggets
item.description: Michael Scotto: TRADE: The Denver Nuggets will select Tarris Reed Jr. with the No. 26 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and are trading him to the San Antonio Spurs for pick No. 35 and two future second round picks, league sources say.

item.title: Denver is trading No. 26 to the San Antonio Spurs for …
item.description: Shams Charania: Denver is trading No. 26 to the San Antonio Spurs for No. 35 and two future second round picks, sources said. The Spurs pick Tarris Reed Jr. at No. 26.

item.title: "Obviously, it's not a bad thing to hear. I think it's …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Aday Mara on being viewed as a potential Victor Wembanyama stopper: "Obviously, it's not a bad thing to hear. I think it's going to take time for adjustment, but I'm excited. I'm excited to play against him (and) obviously to play in the NBA. But I feel like I'm going to play against him a lot. If it's not in the NBA, it's going to be on the national team. So I'm excited to get ready to play against him."

item.title: Brian Wright on De’Aaron Fox: “The confidence level is …
item.description: Jared Weiss: San Antonio Spurs GM Brian Wright on De’Aaron Fox: “The confidence level is very high.” Wright went on to get into Fox not making excuses before saying, “We have the ultimate faith in De’Aaron and who he is as a player, what he’s been for us, and what he means to this team. And there’s no wavering in that at all.”

item.title: We'll continue to put them in situations for them to …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: More from Brian Wright on Dylan Harper and De'Aaron Fox: "They're are a tough matchup. We'll continue to put them in situations for them to be at their best...They'll learn from this year on how they continue to play off one another and support one another and go from there."

item.title: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in De'Aaron Fox and who he is as a player, what he's been for us, and what he means to this team, and there's no wavering in that at all."

item.description: NBA Base: Aday Mara on being called the "Wemby stopper". “Im ready yeah, I don't know how yet. But I will do it” (Via @SInow)

item.title: Jay Willams says OKC and the Spurs won the draft: …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Jay Willams says OKC and the Spurs won the draft: “They continue to put their chokehold on the NBA. What did we talk about a lot with OKC? How did Chet Holmgren play in the NBA finals? What’s going on with Isaiah Hartenstein when he had go against Wemby? That got a guy named Aday Mara who has a 7’4 wingspan, super talented can give you depth off the bench and give you an extra body to go against Wemby. How about for San Antonio for Wembanyama we talked a lot about who’s the enforc

item.title: Victor Wembanyama arrives at the Louis Vuitton Men’s …
item.description: ?NBA Stat?: Summer plans: Victor Wembanyama arrives at the Louis Vuitton Men’s Show in Paris. #NBA

item.title: Adam Silver reiterated his desire for the NBA to …
item.description: Speaking on The Dan Patrick Show, Adam Silver reiterated his desire for the NBA to become “more NFL-like” and said the Spurs-Knicks Finals this spring was the ultimate indicator that the small-market vs. big-market “storyline” is in decline. “The goal over time has been, and I’ve said it, to be more NFL-like. Where you believe your team has a shot,” Silver said.

item.title: Adam Silver: “Maybe not every single season, but your …
item.description: Adam Silver: “Maybe not every single season, but your view is, regardless of the market size, if your team is well-managed, if you have a little bit of luck in there, you have a good chance to be in the playoffs and potentially be the champion. And so I think what we’ve seen is societal changes; I think that nobody’s going to say that Victor Wembanyama in San Antonio can’t get global attention because it’s a smaller market.”

item.title: Trevon Brazile heads to Denver at No. 35 via Spurs' trade
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The San Antonio Spurs will select Trevon Brazile with the No. 35 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Brazile is going to the Denver Nuggets as part of the Tarris Reed Jr. (No. 26 pick) trade.

item.title: Maliq Brown selected by Spurs at No. 44
item.description: Michael Scotto: The San Antonio Spurs will select Maliq Brown with the No. 44 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The San Antonio Spurs will select Ja’Kobi Gillespie with the No. 42 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.description: Kevin O'Connor: "Maybe his former teams." One of the most fascinating comments of the night by @BobbyMarks42 regarding which teams could be interested in trading for Kawhi Leonard. Now, I can't imagine it's the Spurs. But the Raptors? Yeah. Sign me up for Ingram + whatever it takes for Kawhi.

item.title: Re: the video going around from the Nuggets draft room …
item.description: Vinny Benedetto: Re: the video going around from the Nuggets draft room last night, it sounds there was some miscommunication between Denver and San Antonio as the Nuggets' clock was on the verge of expiring.

item.title: Barack Obama: "I think Victor Wembanyama will end up …
item.description: Barack Obama: "I think Victor Wembanyama will end up being as good as we are projected. I watched him at the All-Star game. I was sitting there, first time I'd seen him live, and I don't remember somebody that that big moving like that. He's got to figure out what his go-to move is. He's got to put on some weight, he's got to work on his conditioning. Because he kept, you could tell he got tired, partly because it just looks to me like playing defense today is so much harder because you have to

item.title: Kawhi Leonard would be willing to sign extensions with Raptors, Spurs
item.description: While the LA Clippers are undeniably determined to keep hold of Kawhi Leonard — and with Leonard's clear first choice known to be staying with them in his beloved Southern California — league sources say that Toronto has registered interest in trying to re-acquire the engine of the lone championship team in franchise history should Leonard become truly available.

item.title: Toronto and San Antonio
item.description: Sources close to the situation tell The Stein Line that, when it comes to potential trade talks, there are only two other teams that Leonard would consider signing an extension with: The two teams he played for before becoming a Clipper. Toronto and San Antonio.

item.title: Ja’Kobi Gillespie says he’s already envisioned feeding …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Undersized guard Ja’Kobi Gillespie says he’s already envisioned feeding Victor Wembanyama for lob dunks: “Yeah, for sure. Just throw it up there. That’s what it looks like.”

item.title: Jayden Quaintance said Spurs continue to stress “their …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Jayden Quaintance said Spurs continue to stress “their priority is to make me 100 percent healthy.” He also said he doesn’t know yet whether he’ll be able to participate in Summer League.

item.title: Tarris Reed Jr. called losing the national title …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Tarris Reed Jr. called losing the national title “probably the toughest moment of my life” and that both he and his new teammates will have chips on their shoulders next season. “I feel like that's going to be huge for us next year,” he said.

item.title: Tyon Grant-Foster will join the San Antonio Spurs for …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Gonzaga’s Tyon Grant-Foster will join the San Antonio Spurs for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The rangy, defensively disruptive 6’7” swingman was recently green lit by the NBA’s fitness-to-play panel. Finally cleared to pursue his NBA dream.

item.title: Nikola Vucevic blasts Europeans for calling the NBA 'just a show'
item.description: Nikola Vucevic: 'What irritates me is that people from Europe say the NBA is just a show. If people had watched Boston or Oklahoma City or San Antonio all year, they would realize that NBA teams play defense. It looks different because the players are so good and so powerful and talented that everything they do looks so easy and beautiful. It’s like watching a Formula 1 driver; they take corners and brake without a problem, but if you tried to do it, it wouldn't go so easily. In the NBA, they le

item.title: One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers …
item.description: There are mixed signals around the NBA on the subject of his availability and the possibility of him being moved, with reports circulating that the two-time Finals MVP would be open to reuniting with his former teams, San Antonio and Toronto, though the Houston Rockets could also be part of that mix, per one source. One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers are absolutely not moving him, preferring to let the last year of his contract play out to preserve future flexibility, but stil

item.title: Nets, Spurs interested in signing Rui Hachimura
item.description: Brooklyn keeps coming up as a likely suitor, too, for the Lakers' Rui Hachimura, league sources say. The Nets and Spurs are two teams we've heard have interest in adding Hachimura's services, while the Spurs are also faced with resolving Harrison Barnes' future as the veteran forward heads to unrestricted free agency.

item.title: Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Per source, veteran guard Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract with the Spurs last season, is expected to be added to the Celtics’ summer league roster.

item.title: Cavs, 76eres, Lakers, Spurs, Suns, Pistons, Blazers among teams interested in Dean Wade?
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade is expected to have various suitors in free agency, including the Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, Los Angeles Lakers, San Antonio Spurs, Phoenix Suns, Detroit Pistons, and Portland Trail Blazers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Spurs, Magic and Sixers interested in John Collins
item.description: League sources say John Collins certainly has fans in Minnesota, but it appears unlikely that the Timberwolves will have sufficient spending power to pursue the scoring forward, who spent last season with the Clippers. Collins is also expected to draw interest from Orlando, San Antonio and Philadelphia once free agency begins. The 76ers and Western Conference champion Spurs continue to get linked in free agency projections to the top forwards in the marketplace.

item.title: Clippers exploring re-signing John Collins amid interest from Spurs, Magic, and 76ers
item.description: We're also hearing that the Clippers might elect to try to re-sign forward John Collins, who as The Stein Line previously reported is expected to draw interest from the likes of San Antonio, Orlando and Philadelphia once free agency formally begins Tuesday night at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Keith Palek signed a summer league contract with the …
item.description: Keith Palek signed a summer league contract with the Knicks on June 25, 12 days after the Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs in the NBA Finals to end a 53-year Larry O’Brien trophy drought in Manhattan.

item.title: Julian Champagnie plans to sign three-year deal to stay with Spurs
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: San Antonio Spurs forward Julian Champagnie intends to sign a new three-year, $45 million contract with the franchise through 2028-29, sources tell ESPN. The Spurs declined Champagnie’s $3 million team option to give him a new deal. Waived three years ago, Champagnie has become a vital part of the Spurs core.

item.title: Harrison Barnes staying put in San Antonio
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Harrison Barnes intends to sign a one-year, $8 million deal to stay with the San Antonio Spurs, sources tell ESPN. The Spurs valued Barnes' leadership and production as he enters a third season in San Antonio, finalizing a new deal with agent Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management on Monday night.

item.title: San Antonio Spurs tendered two-way qualifying offers …
item.description: Keith Smith: The San Antonio Spurs tendered two-way qualifying offers to Harrison Ingram and David Jones Garcia, a league source told @spotrac. Both players will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: Harrison Ingram has a standard qualifying offer
item.description: Keith Smith: Correction: Harrison Ingram has a standard qualifying offer.

item.title: Coach Corliss Williamson says Jayden Quaintance will …
item.description: Matthew Tynan: Update from Summer Spurs practice: Coach Corliss Williamson says Jayden Quaintance will be traveling with the team throughout Summer League but will not play. Carter Bryant will be playing, but only in Las Vegas.

item.title: OG Anunoby's game-winning ball from Finals Game 4 returned to the Knicks, won't be up for auction
item.description: The ball that New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby tipped in to seal the biggest comeback in NBA Finals history and win Game 4 against the San Antonio Spurs was returned to the Knicks shortly before it was scheduled to hit the Sotheby’s auction block Tuesday.

item.title: "It's an opportunity for him to explore his game a …
item.description: The Spurs also want Bryant to spread his wings offensively this summer. Williamson said the 14th overall pick of the 2025 NBA Draft will only see action in Las Vegas, where the Spurs will play at least five games after kicking off the summer circuit with three games at the California Classic in San Francisco. It all begins against the Miami Heat at 7 p.m. Friday at Chase Center, home of the Golden State Warriors. "It's an opportunity for him to explore his game a little bit without having to wor

item.title: "I was supposed to coach Summer League last year in …
item.description: Williams said he's "pretty excited" about serving as a Summer League coach for the first time in his career. "It seemed like every time I had the opportunity to be a head coach for Summer League I either took another job or unfortunately early in my career (staffs he was a part of) got fired in a couple of situations," he said. "I was supposed to coach Summer League last year in Minnesota, but I was able to come here, so I'm definitely excited about this chance."

item.title: Pistons interested in Rui Hachimura
item.description: Lakers free agent Rui Hachimura, Clippers free agent John Collins and Pistons free agent Tobias Harris are also known to be on San Antonio's list. Hachimura, for that matter, is also believed to still be drawing interest from Brooklyn and Detroit and is regarded as increasingly likely to join James. Smart and Phoenix-bound Luke Kennard out the exit door in Lakerland.

item.title: The Clippers will create a trade exception by helping …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers will create a trade exception by helping facilitate John Collins’ sign-and-trade to Detroit, sources say. Collins’ three-year, $51 million contract with the Pistons is only guaranteed for the first season, as @omarisankofa reported. I’m told Detroit wasn’t willing to offer Tobias Harris more than one guaranteed season, either. With the Lakers business today, let’s see if Harris or Rui Hachimura end up signing into the Spurs’ open forward spot.

item.title: Tobias Harris joins Spurs on a two-year deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: Breaking: Tobias Harris and the San Antonio Spurs have agreed to a 2-year, $31 million deal, league sources say. Agent Torrel Harris and the Spurs worked together to finalize the deal.

item.title: Spurs not expected to go after LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Spurs’ deal with Tobias Harris slams the point home, but league sources say San Antonio is not expected to pursue LeBron James. @BennettDurando reported that Denver, by contrast, IS joining Golden State, Cleveland and Miami in this chase. More NBA: tinyurl.com/pebetpkn

item.title: So, Boston either comes away with a decent pick …
item.description: Keith Smith: For full clarity, here are the various options for the 2028 swap/pick the Celtics got in the trade with the Sixers: 1. If LAC is 1-16 AND PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of LAC/PHI/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 2. If LAC is 1-16 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS gets the best of LAC/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 3. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of PHI/lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 4. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS keeps lesser of

item.title: The primary reason the Lakers likely chose Kessler …
item.description: The primary reason the Lakers likely chose Kessler over offering a max deal to Detroit Pistons restricted free agent Jalen Duren is that Kessler is one of the NBA’s best rim protectors. He’s the kind of interior presence a team needs if it’s going to employ Dončić and Reaves on the perimeter as primary playmakers. In 2024-25, Kessler’s last full(ish) season, he contested 7.5 shots per game at the rim, according to NBA.com, which tied for the fifth-most in the league. On those shots, he allowed

item.title: New York (+850), jumping into third place on the title …
item.description: Sitting firmly in third place in the NBA Finals winner market since the start of the offseason, behind the San Antonio Spurs (+270) and Oklahoma City Thunder (+270), Boston dipped from +650 odds before the trade to +1100 odds after. New York (+850), jumping into third place on the title odds board past the rival Celtics, is now back in the Eastern Conference driver’s seat despite a quiet offseason focused on re-signing role players. Even with the Knicks losing backup center and offensive rebound

item.title: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated:
item.description: Kevin Chouinard: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated: Utah 7/4 UTA 5:00 ET Prime/ESPN U 7/6 OKC 7:00 ET ESPN/Prime 7/7 MEM 7:00 ET Prime/ESPN U Vegas 7/9 SAS 4:30 ET ESPN 2 7/11 BKN 8:00 ET ESPN 7/13 BOS 6:00 ET Prime 7/16 MEM 8:00 ET Prime

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Tobias Harris says "Urgency to win a championship" is why he joined Spurs
item.description: As for what factored ultimately in his decision to choose the Spurs, Harris told HoopsHype via text message, “The urgency to win a championship. It felt like both our goals aligned.”

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Tobias Harris says "urgency to win a championship" is why he joined Spurs
item.description: As for what factored ultimately in his decision to choose the Spurs, Harris told HoopsHype via text message, “The urgency to win a championship. It felt like both our goals aligned.”

item.title: Billy Donovan joins Spurs as lead assistant coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Billy Donovan has agreed to become the lead assistant coach for the San Antonio Spurs and head coach Mitch Johnson, sources tell me and @PeteThamel. After 11 seasons as a head coach in Chicago and Oklahoma City, Donovan accepts the position with the Western Conference champions.

item.title: Donovan's son, Billy III, coached w/Mitch in '17 in G …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Billy Donovan & Mitch Johnson have a strong personal relationship. Donovan's son, Billy III, coached w/Mitch in '17 in G League w/the Austin Spurs. Also worth noting Mitch J. & GM Brian Wright are totally in lockstep. Spurs will need 2 replace 3-4 folks off last season's staff.

item.title: Hachimura was very much in play for San Antonio before …
item.description: Sources say Hachimura was very much in play for San Antonio before Tobias Harris rejected an offer to return to the Detroit Pistons. Harris and the Spurs swiftly agreed to a two-year, $31 million deal after San Antonio and Brooklyn were most frequently mentioned as likely Hachimura suitors before free agency officially began Tuesday at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Timberwolves bring Trey Lyles back to the NBA
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Trey Lyles have agreed to a 1-year deal. Lyles returns to the NBA after playing for Real Madrid. He’s averaged 7.6 points and 4.3 rebounds with the Jazz, Nuggets, Spurs, Pistons and Kings.

item.title: When Bron’s IQ is sometimes more than the coach’s, it …
item.description: I want to throw a little curveball at you, and I don’t know if you think I’m crazy by saying this, but I’ve thrown out the idea of the Spurs — if he takes the veteran’s minimum — being a great landing spot for LeBron, because they already have all the pieces. They just kind of need that veteran to stabilize everything, and he would be the guy that would help him get over the hump, and it would help him get closer to Jordan, if not match Jordan by the time his career is over. What are your overal

item.title: For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: RJ Barrett, Zach Edey, Dwight Powell and Kelly Olynyk are here watching Canada-Puerto Rico. For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), they're not playing this summer but maintain spots in the country's player pool. Expected to be in the mix for the World Cup next summer.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo on who is winning one-on-one matchup, him or Victor Wembanyama: Me, all day
item.description: Heat Central: Neon: Who you got in a 1v1 you or Wemby? Giannis: Me. All day. All day

item.title: Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized …
item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

item.title: Lakers second-year forward Adou Thiero missed the …
item.description: Lakers second-year forward Adou Thiero missed the Lakers’ California Classic summer league finale Monday because of a right wrist injury. The 22-year-old suffered the injury while falling in the Lakers’ sudden-death, double-overtime win Sunday, Lakers summer league coach Ty Abbott said Monday, and wore a brace on his right wrist while cheering the Lakers to an 88-84 win over the San Antonio Spurs at Chase Center. Thiero’s status for Las Vegas summer league games, which begin Friday for the Laker

item.title: Bexar County commissioners approved that the San …
item.description: Bexar County commissioners approved that the San Antonio Spurs can play up to four regular-season home games away from the Frost Bank Center for the next two seasons, which will likely include games in Austin and Europe. Though playing home games outside of their actual home arena has become a regular occurrence for the Spurs over the past four seasons, the new agreement allows for the most home-away-from-home games in several years and without the same restrictions as the most recent agreement.

item.title: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA …
item.description: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA champions have won without luring accomplished free agents. Instead, the teams have largely drafted stars (Bucks, Warriors, Nuggets, Celtics) or assembled lineups by picking up players other teams were willing to part with (Knicks). The Thunder have built a perennial powerhouse by doing a bit of both, while the Spurs are tied with OKC as 2027/28 favorites thanks to a run of draft success.

item.title: Project B, the startup basketball league set to launch …
item.description: Project B, the startup basketball league set to launch next winter, has hired Landry Fields to oversee the men’s basketball division. Fields, 38, is a former Atlanta Hawks general manager who once played for the New York Knicks. Fields was the Hawks’ GM from 2022 through 2025 and became an executive for the San Antonio Spurs organization after his five-year NBA playing career. He has been the president of league operations for Overtime Elite, a men’s league for players ages 16-20, since January

item.title: Jacob Tobey is out as the Spurs’ lead play-by-play …
item.description: Jacob Tobey is out as the Spurs’ lead play-by-play announcer after he was accused on social media of cheating on his girlfriend with the sister of a player on the team, sources told Front Office Sports.

item.title: Earlier this week, multiple posts accusing Tobey of …
item.description: Earlier this week, multiple posts accusing Tobey of the affair were shared on his Instagram account. The posts went viral across multiple social media platforms. In a subsequent post on her own social media account, Tobey’s girlfriend indicated that she was responsible for the posts on his account and that their relationship is now over.

item.title: His “NBA Finals Debut” jersey, the guard wore during …
item.description: Jalen Brunson’s meteoric rise within the sports collectibles and memorabilia space since the New York Knicks won the 2026 NBA Finals has reached another level. A seven-figure level. His “NBA Finals Debut” jersey, the guard wore during Game 1 against the San Antonio Spurs, sold for $1.024 million through an auction via Sotheby’s on Wednesday night, a new record for a piece of Knicks memorabilia.

item.title: In South Korea, however, he is basically Steph Curry. …
item.description: Lee is aiming to cash in on his long-shot opportunity from long distance. And his aim is usually true. “We all know he can shoot the basketball,” Spurs Summer League coach Corliss Williamson said. Having spent the past two seasons playing professionally overseas, Lee is a relative unknown among the American basketball-watching public. In South Korea, however, he is basically Steph Curry. Last November, Lee set the FIBA single-game record for 3-pointers in a World Cup qualifier with nine, set aga

item.title: Jacob Tobey is out as the Spurs’ lead play-by-play …
item.description: Jacob Tobey is out as the Spurs’ lead play-by-play announcer after he was accused on social media of cheating on his girlfriend with the sister of a player on the team, sources told Front Office Sports. Reps for the Spurs and Tobey could not immediately be reached for comment. Tobey, a talented young announcer who has called Spurs games since 2024, was on FOS’s list of rising young play-by-play broadcasters last year. Prior to joining the Spurs’ booth, he worked at 9NEWS in Denver and has called
